ja.actions: Support riparian 修復 and water quality improvement along Atlantic-slope 川s, back host 魚 recovery プログラムmes, and 提唱 for stronger Clean Water Act enforcement.

zh.description: The dwarf wedge mussel is a tiny 淡水 mussel once found across dozens of Atlantic-slope 河流s in the eastern United States, now 减少d to a handful of scattered 种群s.
zh.endangerment: Water quality degradation from agricultural and urban runoff, sedimentation, and the decline of host 鱼类 needed for its larval stage have driven dramatic range contr行动.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, fast-to-moderate-flowing streams and 河流s with 稳定 gravel and sand substrates, good water quality, and host 鱼类 — especially tessellated darters and Atlantic salmon — for larval 开发.
zh.actions: Support riparian 恢复 and water quality improvement along Atlantic-slope 河流s, back host 鱼类 recovery 项目mes, and 倡导 for stronger Clean Water Act enforcement.

ar.description: The dwarf wedge mussel is a tiny مياه عذبة mussel once found across dozens of Atlantic-slope نهرs in the eastern United States, now يقللd to a handful of scattered عدد السكانs.
ar.endangerment: Water quality degradation from agricultural and urban runoff, sedimentation, and the decline of host أسماك needed for its larval stage have driven dramatic range contrإجراء.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, fast-to-moderate-flowing streams and نهرs with مستقر gravel and sand substrates, good water quality, and host أسماك — especially tessellated darters and Atlantic salmon — for larval التطوير.
ar.actions: Support riparian إعادة and water quality improvement along Atlantic-slope نهرs, back host أسماك recovery برنامجmes, and يدافع for stronger Clean Water Act enforcement.

hi.description: The dwarf wedge mussel is a tiny मीठा पानी mussel once found across dozens of Atlantic-slope नदीs in the eastern United States, now कमd to a handful of scattered जनसंख्याs.
hi.endangerment: Water quality degradation from agricultural and urban runoff, sedimentation, and the decline of host मछली needed for its larval stage have driven dramatic range contrकार्रवाई.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, fast-to-moderate-flowing streams and नदीs with स्थिर gravel and sand substrates, good water quality, and host मछली — especially tessellated darters and Atlantic salmon — for larval विकास.
hi.actions: Support riparian पुनर्स्थापना and water quality improvement along Atlantic-slope नदीs, back host मछली recovery कार्यक्रमmes, and समर्थन for stronger Clean Water Act enforcement.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The Sumatran rhinoceros is the smallest and most ancient living rhinoceros, covered in reddish-brown hair, surviving in fragmented forest patches of Sumatra and Borneo.
en.endangerment: Relentless poaching for horn and massive deforestation have reduced the wild population to fewer than 80 individuals scattered across isolated fragments.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense tropical rainforest with salt licks, wallowing pools, and sufficient browse plants such as fig, mango, and bamboo.
en.actions: Support the Sumatran Rhino Rescue programme, advocate for forest corridor restoration, and push for stronger anti-poaching enforcement in Sumatra.

es.description: El rinoceronte de Sumatra es el rinoceronte vivo más pequeño y antiguo, cubierto de pelo de color marrón rojizo, que sobrevive en fragmentos de bosque de Sumatra y Borneo.
es.endangerment: La caza furtiva implacable por cuerno y la deforestación masiva han reducido la población silvestre a menos de 80 individuos dispersos en fragmentos aislados.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical denso con salinas, pozas para revolcarse y suficientes plantas de ramoneo como higos, mangos y bambú.
es.actions: Apoya el programa de Rescate de Rinocerontes de Sumatra, aboga por la restauración de corredores forestales y presiona por una aplicación más estricta contra la caza furtiva en Sumatra.

pt.description: O rinoceronte-de-sumatra é o menor e mais antigo rinoceronte vivo, coberto de pelo marrom-avermelhado, sobrevivendo em fragmentos de floresta de Sumatra e Borneo.
pt.endangerment: O braconagem implacável pelo chifre e o desmatamento massivo reduziram a população selvagem a menos de 80 indivíduos dispersos em fragmentos isolados.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical densa com lameiros salinos, poças para se espojar e plantas de pastejo suficientes como figos, mangas e bambu.
pt.actions: Apoie o programa de Resgate do Rinoceronte de Sumatra, defenda a restauração de corredores florestais e pressione por uma aplicação antibraconagem mais rigorosa em Sumatra.

fr.description: Le rhinocéros de Sumatra est le plus petit et le plus ancien rhinocéros vivant, couvert d'un pelage brun-rougeâtre, survivant dans des fragments forestiers de Sumatra et Bornéo.
fr.endangerment: Le braconnage implacable pour la corne et la déforestation massive ont réduit la population sauvage à moins de 80 individus dispersés dans des fragments isolés.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale dense avec des pierres à sel, des mares de vautrage et suffisamment de plantes à brouter comme les figuiers, les manguiers et les bambous.
fr.actions: Soutenez le programme de Sauvetage du Rhinocéros de Sumatra, plaidez pour la restauration des corridors forestiers et poussez à une application antibraconnage plus stricte à Sumatra.

ja.description: The Sumatran rhinoceros is the 最小 and most 古代 living rhinoceros, covered in reddish-brown hair, surviving in fragmented 森林 patches of Sumatra and Borneo.
ja.endangerment: Relentless 密猟 for horn and massive 森林伐採 have 削減d the 野生 個体数 to 未満 80 個体 scattered across isolated fragments.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dense 熱帯 rain森林 with salt licks, wallowing pools, and sufficient browse 植物 such as fig, maNGO, and bamboo.
ja.actions: Support the Sumatran Rhino Rescue プログラムme, 提唱 for 森林 corridor 修復, and push for stronger anti-密猟 enforcement in Sumatra.

zh.description: The Sumatran rhinoceros is the 最小 and most 古代 living rhinoceros, covered in reddish-brown hair, surviving in fragmented 森林 patches of Sumatra and Borneo.
zh.endangerment: Relentless 偷猎 for horn and massive 森林砍伐 have 减少d the 野生 种群 to 少于 80 个体 scattered across isolated fragments.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Dense 热带 rain森林 with salt licks, wallowing pools, and sufficient browse 植物 such as fig, ma非政府组织, and bamboo.
zh.actions: Support the Sumatran Rhino Rescue 项目me, 倡导 for 森林 corridor 恢复, and push for stronger anti-偷猎 enforcement in Sumatra.

ar.description: The Sumatran rhinoceros is the أصغر and most قديم living rhinoceros, covered in reddish-brown hair, surviving in fragmented غابة patches of Sumatra and Borneo.
ar.endangerment: Relentless الصيد الجائر for horn and massive إزالة الغابات have يقللd the بري عدد السكان to أقل من 80 أفراد scattered across isolated fragments.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Dense استوائي rainغابة with salt licks, wallowing pools, and sufficient browse نباتات such as fig, maمنظمة غير حكومية, and bamboo.
ar.actions: Support the Sumatran Rhino Rescue برنامجme, يدافع for غابة corridor إعادة, and push for stronger anti-الصيد الجائر enforcement in Sumatra.

hi.description: The Sumatran rhinoceros is the सबसे छोटा and most प्राचीन living rhinoceros, covered in reddish-brown hair, surviving in fragmented वन patches of Sumatra and Borneo.
hi.endangerment: Relentless अवैध शिकार for horn and massive वनों की कटाई have कमd the वन्य जनसंख्या to कम से कम 80 व्यक्ति scattered across isolated fragments.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Dense उष्णकटिबंधीय rainवन with salt licks, wallowing pools, and sufficient browse पौधे such as fig, maएनजीओ, and bamboo.
hi.actions: Support the Sumatran Rhino Rescue कार्यक्रमme, समर्थन for वन corridor पुनर्स्थापना, and push for stronger anti-अवैध शिकार enforcement in Sumatra.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The eastern lowland gorilla, or Grauer's gorilla, is the largest subspecies of gorilla, found only in the lowland tropical forests of eastern Democratic Republic of Congo.
en.endangerment: Armed conflict, mining, and bushmeat hunting have decimated populations; surveys estimate a decline of over 70% since the 1990s.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane rainforest in eastern DRC with diverse fruit sources, mineral licks, and freedom from human conflict.
en.actions: Support conservation organisations working in conflict zones in DRC, advocate for responsible sourcing of minerals from the Congo Basin, and back ranger programmes.

es.description: El gorila de tierras bajas del este, o gorila de Grauer, es la subespecie más grande de gorila, encontrado solo en los bosques tropicales de tierras bajas del este de la República Democrática del Congo.
es.endangerment: El conflicto armado, la minería y la caza de animales silvestres han diezmado las poblaciones; las encuestas estiman un declive de más del 70% desde la década de 1990.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque lluvioso de tierras bajas y montaña intacto en el este de la RDC con diversas fuentes de fruta, laminas minerales y libertad del conflicto humano.
es.actions: Apoya las organizaciones de conservación que trabajan en zonas de conflicto en la RDC, aboga por el abastecimiento responsable de minerales de la cuenca del Congo y respalda los programas de guardabosques.

pt.description: O gorila-de-planície-oriental, ou gorila de Grauer, é a maior subespécie de gorila, encontrada apenas nas florestas tropicais de planície do leste da República Democrática do Congo.
pt.endangerment: Conflitos armados, mineração e caça de animais silvestres dizimaram as populações; pesquisas estimam um declínio de mais de 70% desde a década de 1990.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de planície e montana intacta no leste da RDC com diversas fontes de frutas, lameiros minerais e liberdade de conflitos humanos.
pt.actions: Apoie organizações de conservação que trabalham em zonas de conflito na RDC, defenda o abastecimento responsável de minerais da Bacia do Congo e respalda os programas de guardas florestais.

fr.description: Le gorille de plaine de l'Est, ou gorille de Grauer, est la plus grande sous-espèce de gorille, que l'on ne trouve que dans les forêts tropicales de basse altitude de l'est de la RDC.
fr.endangerment: Les conflits armés, l'exploitation minière et le commerce de viande de brousse ont décimé les populations ; les enquêtes estiment un déclin de plus de 70% depuis les années 1990.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt pluviale de plaine et de montagne intacte dans l'est de la RDC avec des sources de fruits diversifiées, des lamines minérales et une absence de conflits humains.
fr.actions: Soutenez les organisations de conservation travaillant dans les zones de conflit en RDC, plaidez pour un approvisionnement responsable en minéraux du bassin du Congo et appuyez les programmes de rangers.

ja.description: The eastern lowland gorilla, or Grauer's gorilla, is the 最大 sub種 of gorilla, 見つけた only in the lowland 熱帯 森林s of eastern Democratic Republic of CoNGO.
ja.endangerment: Armed conflict, 採掘, and bushmeat 狩猟 have decimated 個体数s; surveys estimate a decline of over 70% since the 1990s.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane rain森林 in eastern DRC with diverse fruit sources, mineral licks, and freedom from human conflict.
ja.actions: Support 保全 organisations working in conflict zones in DRC, 提唱 for 責任 sourcing of minerals from the CoNGO Basin, and back ranger プログラムmes.

zh.description: The eastern lowland gorilla, or Grauer's gorilla, is the 最大 sub物种 of gorilla, found only in the lowland 热带 森林s of eastern Democratic Republic of Co非政府组织.
zh.endangerment: Armed conflict, 采矿, and bushmeat 狩猎 have decimated 种群s; surveys estimate a decline of over 70% since the 1990s.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane rain森林 in eastern DRC with diverse fruit sources, mineral licks, and freedom from human conflict.
zh.actions: Support 保护 organisations working in conflict zones in DRC, 倡导 for 责任 sourcing of minerals from the Co非政府组织 Basin, and back ranger 项目mes.

ar.description: The eastern lowland gorilla, or Grauer's gorilla, is the أكبر subالأنواع of gorilla, found only in the lowland استوائي غابةs of eastern Democratic Republic of Coمنظمة غير حكومية.
ar.endangerment: Armed conflict, التعدين, and bushmeat الصيد have decimated عدد السكانs; surveys estimate a decline of over 70% since the 1990s.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane rainغابة in eastern DRC with diverse fruit sources, mineral licks, and freedom from human conflict.
ar.actions: Support الحفظ organisations working in conflict zones in DRC, يدافع for مسؤول sourcing of minerals from the Coمنظمة غير حكومية Basin, and back ranger برنامجmes.

hi.description: The eastern lowland gorilla, or Grauer's gorilla, is the सबसे बड़ा subप्रजातियाँ of gorilla, found only in the lowland उष्णकटिबंधीय वनs of eastern Democratic Republic of Coएनजीओ.
hi.endangerment: Armed conflict, खनन, and bushmeat शिकार have decimated जनसंख्याs; surveys estimate a decline of over 70% since the 1990s.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane rainवन in eastern DRC with diverse fruit sources, mineral licks, and freedom from human conflict.
hi.actions: Support संरक्षण organisations working in conflict zones in DRC, समर्थन for जिम्मेदार sourcing of minerals from the Coएनजीओ Basin, and back ranger कार्यक्रमmes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The black-footed ferret is North America's only native ferret species and one of the most endangered mammals on the continent, dependent almost entirely on prairie dogs for food and shelter.
en.endangerment: Deliberate poisoning of prairie dog colonies eliminated most of its prey base. Sylvatic plague, introduced to North America, now kills both ferrets and prairie dogs.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large intact prairie dog towns on shortgrass prairies of the Great Plains and intermountain basins, free from plague and with sufficient prey density.
en.actions: Support prairie dog habitat protection, back plague vaccine deployment programmes, and fund captive breeding and reintroduction at historical sites.

es.description: El hurón de patas negras es la única especie de hurón nativa de América del Norte y uno de los mamíferos más amenazados del continente, dependiendo casi por completo de las perros de las praderas para alimento y refugio.
es.endangerment: El envenenamiento deliberado de las colonias de perros de las praderas eliminó la mayor parte de su base de presas. La plaga silvática, introducida en América del Norte, ahora mata tanto a hurones como a perros de las praderas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes pueblos intactos de perros de las praderas en praderas de hierba corta de las Grandes Llanuras y cuencas intermontanas, libres de plaga y con suficiente densidad de presas.
es.actions: Apoya la protección del hábitat de perros de las praderas, respalda los programas de implementación de vacunas contra la plaga y financia la reproducción en cautividad y la reintroducción en sitios históricos.

pt.description: O furão-de-pés-negros é a única espécie de furão nativa da América do Norte e um dos mamíferos mais ameaçados do continente, dependendo quase exclusivamente de cães-da-pradaria para alimento e abrigo.
pt.endangerment: O envenenamento deliberado das colônias de cães-da-pradaria eliminou a maior parte de sua base de presas. A praga silvática, introduzida na América do Norte, agora mata tanto furões quanto cães-da-pradaria.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes colônias intactas de cães-da-pradaria em pradarias de gramíneas curtas das Grandes Planícies e bacias intermontanas, livres de praga e com densidade de presas suficiente.
pt.actions: Apoie a proteção do habitat de cães-da-pradaria, respalda os programas de implantação de vacinas contra a praga e finance a criação em cativeiro e a reintrodução em sítios históricos.

fr.description: Le furet à pieds noirs est la seule espèce de furet indigène d'Amérique du Nord et l'un des mammifères les plus menacés du continent, dépendant presque entièrement des chiens de prairie pour sa nourriture et son abri.
fr.endangerment: L'empoisonnement délibéré des colonies de chiens de prairie a éliminé la majeure partie de sa base de proies. La peste sylvatique, introduite en Amérique du Nord, tue maintenant à la fois les furets et les chiens de prairie.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De grandes villes de chiens de prairie intactes dans les prairies à herbes courtes des Grandes Plaines et des bassins intermontagnards, exemptes de plague et avec une densité de proies suffisante.
fr.actions: Soutenez la protection de l'habitat des chiens de prairie, appuyez les programmes de déploiement du vaccin contre la plague et financez l'élevage en captivité et la réintroduction dans les sites historiques.

ja.description: The black-footed ferret is 北アメリカ's only native ferret 種 and one of the most 絶滅危惧 哺乳類 on the continent, 依存 almost entirely on prairie dogs for food and shelter.
ja.endangerment: Deliberate poisoning of prairie dog colonies eliminated most of its prey base. Sylvatic plague, introduced to 北アメリカ, now kills both ferrets and prairie dogs.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Large intact prairie dog towns on shortgrass prairies of the Great Plains and inter山 basins, free from plague and with sufficient prey density.
ja.actions: Support prairie dog habitat 保護ion, back plague vaccine deployment プログラムmes, and fund 人死亡 and 再導入 at 歴史的 sites.

zh.description: The black-footed ferret is 北美洲's only native ferret 物种 and one of the most 濒危 哺乳动物 on the continent, 依赖 almost entirely on prairie dogs for food and shelter.
zh.endangerment: Deliberate poisoning of prairie dog colonies eliminated most of its prey base. Sylvatic plague, introduced to 北美洲, now kills both ferrets and prairie dogs.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Large intact prairie dog towns on shortgrass prairies of the Great Plains and inter山脉 basins, free from plague and with sufficient prey density.
zh.actions: Support prairie dog habitat 保护ion, back plague vaccine deployment 项目mes, and fund 圈养繁殖 and 重新引入 at historical sites.

ar.description: The black-footed ferret is أمريكا الشمالية's only native ferret الأنواع and one of the most مهدد ثدييات on the continent, اعتماد almost entirely on prairie dogs for food and shelter.
ar.endangerment: Deliberate poisoning of prairie dog colonies eliminated most of its prey base. Sylvatic plague, introduced to أمريكا الشمالية, now kills both ferrets and prairie dogs.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Large intact prairie dog towns on shortgrass prairies of the Great Plains and interجبل basins, free from plague and with sufficient prey density.
ar.actions: Support prairie dog habitat يحميion, back plague vaccine deployment برنامجmes, and fund التكاثر في الأسر and إعادة الإدخال at historical sites.

hi.description: The black-footed ferret is उत्तरी अमेरिका's only native ferret प्रजातियाँ and one of the most लुप्तप्राय स्तनधारी on the continent, निर्भर almost entirely on prairie dogs for food and shelter.
hi.endangerment: Deliberate poisoning of prairie dog colonies eliminated most of its prey base. Sylvatic plague, introduced to उत्तरी अमेरिका, now kills both ferrets and prairie dogs.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Large intact prairie dog towns on shortgrass prairies of the Great Plains and interपहाड़ basins, free from plague and with sufficient prey density.
hi.actions: Support prairie dog habitat सुरक्षाion, back plague vaccine deployment कार्यक्रमmes, and fund कैद प्रजनन and पुनर्प्रवर्तन at historical sites.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The addax is a pale desert antelope with spiralling horns, exquisitely adapted to survive in the Sahara without drinking water, obtaining moisture from the sparse plants it eats.
en.endangerment: Unregulated hunting using motor vehicles decimated wild populations; oil exploration activity in Niger further disturbed remaining animals.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Remote sandy and stony Saharan desert with sparse grass and herbs, far from human settlement and vehicle traffic.
en.actions: Support Niger's Termit and Tin Toumma reserve, back reintroduction efforts in Tunisia and Morocco, and advocate for anti-poaching enforcement across the Sahel.

es.description: El adax es un pálido antílope del desierto con cuernos en espiral, exquisitamente adaptado para sobrevivir en el Sahara sin beber agua, obteniendo la humedad de las escasas plantas que come.
es.endangerment: La caza no regulada con vehículos motorizados diezmó las poblaciones silvestres; la actividad de exploración petrolera en Níger perturbó aún más a los animales restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Desierto sahariano arenoso y pedregoso remoto con hierba y hierbas dispersas, lejos del asentamiento humano y del tráfico de vehículos.
es.actions: Apoya la reserva de Termit y Tin Toumma de Níger, respalda los esfuerzos de reintroducción en Túnez y Marruecos y aboga por la aplicación de medidas anticontrabando en todo el Sahel.

pt.description: O adax é um pálido antílope do deserto com chifres em espiral, exquisitamente adaptado para sobreviver no Saara sem beber água, obtendo a umidade das esparsas plantas que come.
pt.endangerment: A caça não regulamentada com veículos motorizados dizimou as populações selvagens; a atividade de exploração de petróleo no Níger perturbou ainda mais os animais restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Deserto sahariano arenoso e pedregoso remoto com gramíneas e ervas esparsas, longe do assentamento humano e do tráfego de veículos.
pt.actions: Apoie a reserva de Termit e Tin Toumma do Níger, respalda os esforços de reintrodução na Tunísia e em Marrocos e defenda a aplicação de medidas antibraconagem em todo o Sahel.

fr.description: L'addax est une pâle antilope du désert aux cornes en spirale, exquisément adaptée pour survivre dans le Sahara sans boire d'eau, obtenant l'humidité des rares plantes qu'elle consomme.
fr.endangerment: La chasse non réglementée avec des véhicules motorisés a décimé les populations sauvages ; l'activité d'exploration pétrolière au Niger a perturbé davantage les animaux restants.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Un désert saharien sableux et caillouteux isolé avec des graminées et des herbes éparses, loin des établissements humains et du trafic de véhicules.
fr.actions: Soutenez la réserve de Termit et Tin Toumma au Niger, appuyez les efforts de réintroduction en Tunisie et au Maroc et plaidez pour l'application des mesures antibraconnage dans tout le Sahel.

ja.description: The addax is a pale desert antelope with spiralling horns, exquisitely adapted to survive in the Sahara without drinking water, obtaining moisture from the sparse 植物 it eats.
ja.endangerment: Unregulated 狩猟 using motor vehicles decimated 野生 個体数s; oil exploration activity in Niger further disturbed remaining 動物.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Remote sandy and stony Saharan desert with sparse grass and herbs, far from human settlement and vehicle traffic.
ja.actions: Support Niger's Termit and Tin Toumma reserve, back 再導入 努力s in Tunisia and Morocco, and 提唱 for anti-密猟 enforcement across the Sahel.

zh.endangerment: Unregulated 狩猎 using motor vehicles decimated 野生 种群s; oil exploration activity in Niger further disturbed remaining 动物.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Remote sandy and stony Saharan desert with sparse grass and herbs, far from human settlement and vehicle traffic.
zh.actions: Support Niger's Termit and Tin Toumma reserve, back 重新引入 努力s in Tunisia and Morocco, and 倡导 for anti-偷猎 enforcement across the Sahel.

ar.description: The addax is a pale desert antelope with spiralling horns, exquisitely adapted to survive in the Sahara without drinking water, obtaining moisture from the sparse نباتات it eats.
ar.endangerment: Unregulated الصيد using motor vehicles decimated بري عدد السكانs; oil exploration activity in Niger further disturbed remaining حيوانات.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Remote sandy and stony Saharan desert with sparse grass and herbs, far from human settlement and vehicle traffic.
ar.actions: Support Niger's Termit and Tin Toumma reserve, back إعادة الإدخال جهدs in Tunisia and Morocco, and يدافع for anti-الصيد الجائر enforcement across the Sahel.

hi.description: The addax is a pale desert antelope with spiralling horns, exquisitely adapted to survive in the Sahara without drinking water, obtaining moisture from the sparse पौधे it eats.
hi.endangerment: Unregulated शिकार using motor vehicles decimated वन्य जनसंख्याs; oil exploration activity in Niger further disturbed remaining जानवर.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Remote sandy and stony Saharan desert with sparse grass and herbs, far from human settlement and vehicle traffic.
hi.actions: Support Niger's Termit and Tin Toumma reserve, back पुनर्प्रवर्तन प्रयासs in Tunisia and Morocco, and समर्थन for anti-अवैध शिकार enforcement across the Sahel.

en.description: The pygmy hippopotamus is a small, shy, nocturnal relative of the common hippo, found in the swamps and forests of West Africa, spending much more time on land than its larger cousin.
en.endangerment: Hunting for bushmeat and widespread deforestation across Liberia, Sierra Leone, Guinea, and Côte d'Ivoire have drastically reduced populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense wet forest and swampy areas near rivers in West Africa, with undisturbed vegetation for shelter and aquatic plants for grazing at night.
en.actions: Support protected area management in Sapo National Park and Tai Forest, fund camera trap surveys, and back anti-hunting legislation in Liberia.

es.description: El hipopótamo pigmeo es un pequeño y tímido pariente nocturno del hipopótamo común, encontrado en los pantanos y bosques de África Occidental, que pasa mucho más tiempo en tierra que su primo más grande.
es.endangerment: La caza de animales silvestres para carne y la deforestación generalizada en Liberia, Sierra Leona, Guinea y Côte d'Ivoire han reducido drásticamente las poblaciones.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque húmedo denso y áreas pantanosas cerca de ríos en África Occidental, con vegetación sin perturbaciones para refugio y plantas acuáticas para pastar de noche.
es.actions: Apoya la gestión de áreas protegidas en el Parque Nacional Sapo y el Bosque Taï, financia estudios con cámaras trampa y respalda la legislación anti-caza en Liberia.

pt.description: O hipopótamo-pigmeu é um pequeno e tímido parente noturno do hipopótamo comum, encontrado nos pântanos e florestas da África Ocidental, passando muito mais tempo em terra do que seu primo maior.
pt.endangerment: A caça de animais silvestres para carne e o desmatamento generalizado na Libéria, Serra Leoa, Guiné e Côte d'Ivoire reduziram drasticamente as populações.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta úmida densa e áreas pantanosas próximas a rios na África Ocidental, com vegetação sem perturbações para abrigo e plantas aquáticas para pastoreio à noite.
pt.actions: Apoie a gestão de áreas protegidas no Parque Nacional Sapo e na Floresta Taï, finance pesquisas com armadilhas fotográficas e respalda a legislação anti-caça na Libéria.

fr.description: L'hippopotame pygmée est un petit parent nocturne et timide de l'hippopotame commun, trouvé dans les marécages et les forêts d'Afrique occidentale, passant beaucoup plus de temps à terre que son grand cousin.
fr.endangerment: La chasse de viande de brousse et la déforestation généralisée au Liberia, en Sierra Leone, en Guinée et en Côte d'Ivoire ont considérablement réduit les populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt humide dense et des zones marécageuses près des rivières en Afrique occidentale, avec une végétation non perturbée pour l'abri et des plantes aquatiques pour le pâturage nocturne.
fr.actions: Soutenez la gestion des zones protégées dans le Parc national de Sapo et la Forêt du Taï, financez des enquêtes par caméras-pièges et soutenez la législation anti-chasse au Liberia.

ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dense 湿った 森林 and swampy areas near 川s in 西アフリカ, with undisturbed vegetation for shelter and 水生 植物 for 採草 at night.
ja.actions: Support 保護ed area 管理ment in Sapo National Park and Tai 森林, fund camera trap surveys, and back anti-狩猟 legislation in Liberia.

zh.endangerment: Hunting for bushmeat and widespread 森林砍伐 across Liberia, Sierra Leone, Guinea, and Côte d'Ivoire have drastically 减少d 种群s.

zh.actions: Support 保护ed area 管理ment in Sapo National Park and Tai 森林, fund camera trap surveys, and back anti-狩猎 legislation in Liberia.

ar.description: The pygmy hippopotamus is a صغير, shy, nocturnal relative of the شائع hippo, found in the swamps and غابةs of غرب أفريقيا, spending much more time on land than its larger cousin.
ar.endangerment: Hunting for bushmeat and widespread إزالة الغابات across Liberia, Sierra Leone, Guinea, and Côte d'Ivoire have drastically يقللd عدد السكانs.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Dense مبلل غابة and swampy areas near نهرs in غرب أفريقيا, with undisturbed vegetation for shelter and مائي نباتات for grazing at night.
ar.actions: Support يحميed area يديرment in Sapo National Park and Tai غابة, fund camera trap surveys, and back anti-الصيد legislation in Liberia.

hi.description: The pygmy hippopotamus is a छोटा, shy, nocturnal relative of the सामान्य hippo, found in the swamps and वनs of पश्चिमी अफ्रीका, spending much more time on land than its larger cousin.

hi.ecosystemNeeds: Dense गीला वन and swampy areas near नदीs in पश्चिमी अफ्रीका, with undisturbed vegetation for shelter and जलीय पौधे for grazing at night.
hi.actions: Support सुरक्षाed area प्रबंधनment in Sapo National Park and Tai वन, fund camera trap surveys, and back anti-शिकार legislation in Liberia.

en.description: The aye-aye is the world's largest nocturnal primate, using its elongated middle finger to tap on wood and extract insect larvae, filling the ecological niche of woodpeckers on Madagascar.
en.endangerment: Habitat loss to slash-and-burn agriculture and logging, combined with persecution due to superstitious beliefs that it is an evil omen, threaten remaining populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Mature tropical and dry deciduous forest across Madagascar with large trees harbouring wood-boring insect larvae and sufficient fruit sources.
en.actions: Support community education programmes to counter negative superstitions, fund reforestation in northeast Madagascar, and back protected area expansion.

es.description: El aye-aye es el primate nocturno más grande del mundo, utiliza su alargado dedo medio para golpear la madera y extraer larvas de insectos, ocupando el nicho ecológico de los carpinteros en Madagascar.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de hábitat por la agricultura de tala y quema y la tala, combinada con la persecución debida a creencias supersticiosas de que es un presagio del mal, amenazan a las poblaciones restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical maduro y caducifolio seco en toda Madagascar con grandes árboles que albergan larvas de insectos que perforan la madera y suficientes fuentes de fruta.
es.actions: Apoya programas de educación comunitaria para contrarrestar supersticiones negativas, financia la reforestación en el noreste de Madagascar y respalda la expansión de áreas protegidas.

pt.description: O aye-aye é o maior primata noturno do mundo, usando seu dedo médio alongado para bater na madeira e extrair larvas de insetos, ocupando o nicho ecológico dos pica-paus em Madagascar.
pt.endangerment: A perda de habitat pela agricultura de corte e queima e pelo desmatamento, combinada com a perseguição devida a crenças supersticiosas de que é um presságio do mal, ameaçam as populações restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical madura e caducifólia seca em toda Madagascar com grandes árvores que abrigam larvas de insetos que perfuram a madeira e fontes de fruta suficientes.
pt.actions: Apoie programas de educação comunitária para combater superstições negativas, finance o reflorestamento no nordeste de Madagascar e respalda a expansão de áreas protegidas.

fr.description: L'aye-aye est le plus grand primate nocturne du monde, utilisant son long majeur pour tapoter le bois et extraire les larves d'insectes, occupant la niche écologique des pics à Madagascar.
fr.endangerment: La perte d'habitat due à l'agriculture sur brûlis et à l'exploitation forestière, combinée aux persécutions liées à des croyances superstitieuses selon lesquelles il serait un mauvais présage, menacent les populations restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: La forêt tropicale mature et la forêt caducifoliée sèche de toute Madagascar, avec de grands arbres abritant des larves d'insectes xylophages et des sources de fruits suffisantes.
fr.actions: Soutenez les programmes d'éducation communautaire pour contrer les superstitions négatives, financez le reboisement dans le nord-est de Madagascar et appuyez l'expansion des zones protégées.

hi.description: The aye-aye is the विश्व's सबसे बड़ा nocturnal primate, using its elongated middle finger to tap on wood and extract insect larvae, filling the ecological niche of woodpeckers on Madagascar.
hi.endangerment: Habitat loss to slash-and-burn कृषि and लकड़ी काटना, combined with persecution due to superstitious beliefs that it is an evil omen, threaten remaining जनसंख्याs.

en.description: The hirola, or Hunter's hartebeest, is the world's rarest antelope, restricted to a narrow strip of arid grassland on the Kenya–Somalia border.
en.endangerment: Drought, disease, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and predation pressure have reduced the population to a few hundred animals in the wild.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open short-grass plains and bushland in northeastern Kenya with seasonal waterholes and minimal competition from livestock.
en.actions: Support the Hirola Conservation Programme's community conservancy model, fund predator management, and back wildlife corridor creation in the Tana River region.

es.description: El hirola, o hartebeest de Hunter, es el antílope más raro del mundo, restringido a una estrecha franja de pastizales áridos en la frontera Kenia-Somalia.
es.endangerment: La sequía, las enfermedades, la degradación del hábitat por el sobrepastoreo y la presión depredadora han reducido la población a unos pocos cientos de animales en la naturaleza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Llanuras abiertas de hierba corta y matorrales en el noreste de Kenia con pozas de agua estacionales y mínima competencia del ganado.
es.actions: Apoya el modelo de conservancia comunitaria del Programa de Conservación del Hirola, financia la gestión de depredadores y respalda la creación de corredores de vida silvestre en la región del río Tana.

pt.description: O hirola, ou hartebeest de Hunter, é o antílope mais raro do mundo, restrito a uma estreita faixa de pastagens áridas na fronteira Quênia-Somália.
pt.endangerment: A seca, as doenças, a degradação do habitat pelo superpastoreio e a pressão de predação reduziram a população a algumas centenas de animais na natureza.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planícies abertas de gramíneas curtas e mato no nordeste do Quênia com aguadas sazonais e competição mínima do gado.
pt.actions: Apoie o modelo de conservância comunitária do Programa de Conservação do Hirola, finance o manejo de predadores e respalda a criação de corredores de vida selvagem na região do Rio Tana.

fr.description: L'hirola, ou bubale de Hunter, est la plus rare des antilopes, cantonnée à une étroite bande de prairies arides à la frontière Kenya-Somalie.
fr.endangerment: La sécheresse, les maladies, la dégradation de l'habitat due au surpâturage et la pression des prédateurs ont réduit la population à quelques centaines d'animaux à l'état sauvage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des plaines ouvertes à herbes courtes et des zones arbustives dans le nord-est du Kenya, avec des points d'eau saisonniers et une concurrence minimale du bétail.
fr.actions: Soutenez le modèle de conservancy communautaire du Programme de conservation de l'hirola, financez la gestion des prédateurs et appuyez la création de corridors fauniques dans la région de la rivière Tana.

ja.endangerment: Drought, 病気, habitat degradation from over採草, and 捕食 pressure have 削減d the 個体数 to a few 百 動物 in the 野生.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Open short-grass plains and bushland in northeastern Kenya with 季節 waterholes and 最小限 競争 from livestock.
ja.actions: Support the Hirola Conservation Programme's コミュニティ conservancy model, fund predator 管理ment, and back 野生生物 corridor creation in the Tana 河川 region.

zh.endangerment: Drought, 疾病, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and 捕食 pressure have 减少d the 种群 to a few 百 动物 in the 野生.

ar.description: The hirola, or Hunter's hartebeest, is the العالم's نادرst antelope, restricted to a narrow strip of جاف grassland on the Kenya–Somalia border.
ar.endangerment: Drought, المرض, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and افتراس pressure have يقللd the عدد السكان to a few مئة حيوانات in the بري.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Open short-grass plains and bushland in northeastern Kenya with موسمي waterholes and minimal competition from livestock.
ar.actions: Support the Hirola Conservation Programme's مجتمع conservancy model, fund predator يديرment, and back الحياة البرية corridor creation in the Tana نهر region.

hi.description: The hirola, or Hunter's hartebeest, is the विश्व's दुर्लभst antelope, restricted to a narrow strip of शुष्क grassland on the Kenya–Somalia border.
hi.endangerment: Drought, रोग, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and शिकार pressure have कमd the जनसंख्या to a few सौ जानवर in the वन्य.

en.description: The Ganges river dolphin is functionally blind, navigating entirely by echolocation in the turbid waters of the Ganges, Brahmaputra, and Meghna river systems.
en.endangerment: Bycatch in fishing nets, water pollution, damming of rivers that fragments populations, and hunting for oil have all contributed to severe decline.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Deep, fast-flowing sections of large South Asian rivers with high fish density, undisturbed by heavy commercial vessel traffic.
en.actions: Support India's Ganges River Dolphin sanctuary network, back bycatch mitigation measures, and advocate for stricter industrial pollution controls on the Ganges.

es.description: El delfín del río Ganges es funcionalmente ciego, navegando completamente por ecolocalización en las turbias aguas de los sistemas fluviales del Ganges, Brahmaputra y Meghna.
es.endangerment: La captura incidental en redes de pesca, la contaminación del agua, el represamiento de ríos que fragmenta las poblaciones y la caza para obtener aceite han contribuido a un grave declive.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Secciones profundas y de flujo rápido de grandes ríos del sur de Asia con alta densidad de peces, sin perturbaciones por intenso tráfico comercial de embarcaciones.
es.actions: Apoya la red de santuarios del Delfín del río Ganges de India, respalda medidas de mitigación de la captura incidental y aboga por controles industriales de contaminación más estrictos en el Ganges.

pt.description: O boto-do-ganges é funcionalmente cego, navegando completamente por ecolocalização nas águas turvas dos sistemas fluviais do Ganges, Brahmaputra e Meghna.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental em redes de pesca, a poluição da água, o represamento de rios que fragmenta as populações e a caça para obtenção de óleo contribuíram para um grave declínio.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Seções profundas e de fluxo rápido de grandes rios do sul da Ásia com alta densidade de peixes, sem perturbações pelo intenso tráfego comercial de embarcações.
pt.actions: Apoie a rede de santuários do Boto do Rio Ganges da Índia, respalda medidas de mitigação da captura acidental e defenda controles industriais de poluição mais rigorosos no Ganges.

fr.description: Le dauphin du Gange est fonctionnellement aveugle, naviguant entièrement par écholocation dans les eaux turbides des systèmes fluviaux du Gange, du Brahmapoutre et du Meghna.
fr.endangerment: Les prises accessoires dans les filets de pêche, la pollution de l'eau, les barrages fragmentant les populations et la chasse pour l'huile ont contribué à un grave déclin.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des sections profondes et à débit rapide des grands fleuves d'Asie du Sud avec une haute densité de poissons, sans perturbations liées au trafic commercial intense des navires.
fr.actions: Soutenez le réseau de sanctuaires du dauphin du Gange en Inde, appuyez les mesures d'atténuation des prises accessoires et plaidez pour des contrôles industriels plus stricts de la pollution du Gange.

ja.description: The Ganges 川 dolphin is functionally blind, navigating entirely by echolocation in the turbid waters of the Ganges, Brahmaputra, and Meghna 川 systems.
ja.endangerment: Bycatch in 魚ing nets, water 汚染, damming of 川s that fragments 個体数s, and 狩猟 for oil have all 寄与d to severe decline.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Deep, fast-flowing sections of large 南アジアn 川s with high 魚 density, undisturbed by heavy commercial vessel traffic.
ja.actions: Support India's Ganges 河川 Dolphin sanctuary network, back bycatch mitigation measures, and 提唱 for stricter industrial 汚染 controls on the Ganges.

zh.description: The Ganges 河流 dolphin is functionally blind, navigating entirely by echolocation in the turbid waters of the Ganges, Brahmaputra, and Meghna 河流 systems.
zh.endangerment: Bycatch in 鱼类ing nets, water 污染, damming of 河流s that fragments 种群s, and 狩猎 for oil have all 贡献d to severe decline.

ar.endangerment: Bycatch in أسماكing nets, water التلوث, damming of نهرs that fragments عدد السكانs, and الصيد for oil have all يساهمd to severe decline.

en.description: The greater bamboo lemur is the largest bamboo lemur, endemic to Madagascar and uniquely adapted to eat giant bamboo pith containing toxic cyanide, which it tolerates through special biology.
en.endangerment: Destruction of bamboo and rainforest for slash-and-burn agriculture, logging, and hunting have reduced the species to a few tiny populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Mature humid rainforest with abundant giant bamboo stands in southeastern Madagascar, particularly near Ranomafana and Andringitra.
en.actions: Support Ranomafana National Park management, fund community-based forest protection near bamboo habitat, and back captive breeding insurance populations.

es.description: El lemur bambú mayor es el lemur bambú más grande, endémico de Madagascar y únicamente adaptado para comer la médula de bambú gigante que contiene cianuro tóxico, que tolera a través de una biología especial.
es.endangerment: La destrucción del bambú y del bosque lluvioso para la agricultura de tala y quema, la tala y la caza han reducido la especie a unas pocas poblaciones pequeñas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque lluvioso húmedo maduro con abundantes cañaverales de bambú gigante en el sureste de Madagascar, particularmente cerca de Ranomafana y Andringitra.
es.actions: Apoya la gestión del Parque Nacional Ranomafana, financia la protección forestal comunitaria cerca del hábitat de bambú y respalda las poblaciones de reproducción en cautividad como seguro.

pt.description: O lêmure-de-bambu-maior é o maior lêmure-de-bambu, endêmico de Madagascar e exclusivamente adaptado para comer a medula de bambu gigante contendo cianeto tóxico, que tolera através de biologia especial.
pt.endangerment: A destruição do bambu e da floresta tropical para a agricultura de corte e queima, a extração de madeira e a caça reduziram a espécie a poucas populações minúsculas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical úmida madura com abundantes bambuzais de bambu gigante no sudeste de Madagascar, particularmente perto de Ranomafana e Andringitra.
pt.actions: Apoie a gestão do Parque Nacional de Ranomafana, finance a proteção florestal comunitária perto do habitat de bambu e respalda as populações de criação em cativeiro como seguro.

fr.description: Le grand lémur brun de bambou est le plus grand lémur brun, endémique de Madagascar et uniquement adapté pour manger la moelle de bambou géant contenant du cyanure toxique, qu'il tolère grâce à une biologie spéciale.
fr.endangerment: La destruction du bambou et de la forêt pluviale pour l'agriculture sur brûlis, l'exploitation forestière et la chasse ont réduit l'espèce à quelques minuscules populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt pluviale humide mature avec d'abondants peuplements de bambou géant dans le sud-est de Madagascar, notamment près de Ranomafana et d'Andringitra.
fr.actions: Soutenez la gestion du Parc national de Ranomafana, financez la protection forestière communautaire près de l'habitat de bambou et appuyez les populations d'élevage en captivité à titre d'assurance.

en.description: The Bornean orangutan is one of humanity's closest relatives and the largest arboreal mammal, spending most of its life high in the rainforest canopy of Borneo.
en.endangerment: Industrial-scale deforestation for palm oil, timber, and mining has destroyed more than half of its habitat since 1950, and illegal hunting continues.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large areas of intact lowland and dipterocarp rainforest with abundant fruiting trees, particularly in Sabah, Sarawak, and Kalimantan.
en.actions: Choose certified sustainable palm oil, support Bornean orangutan rehabilitation centres, and advocate for stronger forest protection laws in Indonesia and Malaysia.

es.description: El orangután de Borneo es uno de los parientes más cercanos de la humanidad y el mayor mamífero arborícola, pasando la mayor parte de su vida en las alturas del dosel del bosque lluvioso de Borneo.
es.endangerment: La deforestación a escala industrial para el aceite de palma, la madera y la minería ha destruido más de la mitad de su hábitat desde 1950, y la caza ilegal continúa.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes áreas de bosque lluvioso de tierras bajas y dipterocárpeo intacto con abundantes árboles frutales, especialmente en Sabah, Sarawak y Kalimantan.
es.actions: Elige aceite de palma certificado sostenible, apoya los centros de rehabilitación de orangutanes de Borneo y aboga por leyes más sólidas de protección forestal en Indonesia y Malasia.

pt.description: O orangotango-de-borneo é um dos parentes mais próximos da humanidade e o maior mamífero arborícola, passando a maior parte de sua vida nas alturas do dossel da floresta tropical de Borneo.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento em escala industrial para o óleo de palma, madeira e mineração destruiu mais da metade de seu habitat desde 1950, e a caça ilegal continua.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes áreas de floresta tropical de planície e de dipterocarpo intacta com árvores frutíferas abundantes, especialmente em Sabah, Sarawak e Kalimantan.
pt.actions: Escolha óleo de palma certificado sustentável, apoie os centros de reabilitação de orangotangos de Borneo e defenda leis mais rigorosas de proteção florestal na Indonésia e na Malásia.

fr.description: L'orang-outan de Bornéo est l'un des parents les plus proches de l'humanité et le plus grand mammifère arboricole, passant la majeure partie de sa vie dans les hauteurs de la canopée de la forêt pluviale de Bornéo.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation à grande échelle pour l'huile de palme, le bois et l'exploitation minière a détruit plus de la moitié de son habitat depuis 1950, et la chasse illégale se poursuit.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De grandes zones de forêt pluviale de basse altitude et de diptérocarpacées intacte avec des arbres fruitiers abondants, notamment à Sabah, Sarawak et Kalimantan.
fr.actions: Choisissez de l'huile de palme certifiée durable, soutenez les centres de réhabilitation des orangs-outans de Bornéo et plaidez pour des lois de protection forestière plus strictes en Indonésie et en Malaisie.

en.description: The southern muriqui is South America's largest primate, a peaceful, social monkey known for its long limbs and spider-like agility through Atlantic Forest canopy in Brazil.
en.endangerment: Destruction of Brazil's Atlantic Forest — reduced to less than 12% of its original extent — has fragmented muriqui habitat into tiny isolated patches.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, connected tracts of Atlantic Forest in Minas Gerais and São Paulo states with mature trees, lianas, and abundant foliage for browsing.
en.actions: Support Atlantic Forest restoration initiatives in Brazil, donate to muriqui research stations like RPPN Feliciano Miguel Abdala, and back forest corridor projects.

es.description: El muriquí del sur es el primate más grande de América del Sur, un mono pacífico y social conocido por sus largas extremidades y su agilidad similar a una araña a través del dosel del bosque atlántico en Brasil.
es.endangerment: La destrucción del bosque atlántico de Brasil —reducido a menos del 12% de su extensión original— ha fragmentado el hábitat del muriquí en pequeños parches aislados.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes extensiones conectadas de bosque atlántico en los estados de Minas Gerais y São Paulo con árboles maduros, lianas y abundante follaje para ramonear.
es.actions: Apoya las iniciativas de restauración del bosque atlántico en Brasil, dona a estaciones de investigación de muriquí y respalda proyectos de corredores forestales.

pt.description: O muriqui-do-sul é o maior primata da América do Sul, um macaco pacífico e social conhecido por seus longos membros e agilidade similar a uma aranha pelo dossel da Mata Atlântica no Brasil.
pt.endangerment: A destruição da Mata Atlântica do Brasil — reduzida a menos de 12% de sua extensão original — fragmentou o habitat do muriqui em pequenos fragmentos isolados.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes extensões conectadas de Mata Atlântica nos estados de Minas Gerais e São Paulo com árvores maduras, cipós e folhagem abundante para pastejo.
pt.actions: Apoie as iniciativas de restauração da Mata Atlântica no Brasil, doe para estações de pesquisa de muriqui e respalda projetos de corredores florestais.

fr.description: Le muriqui du Sud est le plus grand primate d'Amérique du Sud, un singe pacifique et sociable connu pour ses longs membres et son agilité d'araignée dans la canopée de la forêt atlantique au Brésil.
fr.endangerment: La destruction de la forêt atlantique du Brésil — réduite à moins de 12% de son étendue d'origine — a fragmenté l'habitat du muriqui en de minuscules îlots isolés.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De vastes étendues connectées de forêt atlantique dans les États du Minas Gerais et de São Paulo, avec des arbres matures, des lianes et un feuillage abondant pour le broutage.
fr.actions: Soutenez les initiatives de restauration de la forêt atlantique au Brésil, faites des dons aux stations de recherche sur le muriqui et appuyez les projets de corridors forestiers.

hi.description: The southern muriqui is दक्षिण अमेरिका's सबसे बड़ा primate, a peaceful, सामाजिक monkey जानेंn for its long limbs and spider-like agility through Atlantic वन canopy in Brazil.

en.description: The Ethiopian wolf is Africa's most endangered carnivore and the world's rarest canid, restricted to the Afroalpine highlands of Ethiopia where it hunts Afroalpine rodents.
en.endangerment: Rabies and canine distemper spreading from domestic dogs have caused devastating epidemics. Agricultural encroachment is shrinking its high-altitude habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open Afroalpine grassland above 3,000 metres in the Ethiopian highlands, particularly the Bale Mountains, with abundant rodent prey and minimal domestic dog contact.
en.actions: Support the Ethiopian Wolf Conservation Programme's vaccination campaigns, back livestock exclusion zones in core wolf habitat, and fund monitoring of disease outbreaks.

es.description: El lobo etíope es el carnívoro más amenazado de África y el cánido más raro del mundo, restringido a las tierras altas afroalpinas de Etiopía donde caza roedores afroalpinos.
es.endangerment: La rabia y el moquillo canino que se propagan desde perros domésticos han causado epidemias devastadoras. La invasión agrícola está reduciendo su hábitat de gran altitud.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizales afroalpinos abiertos por encima de los 3.000 metros en las tierras altas etíopes, especialmente las montañas Bale, con abundantes presas de roedores y contacto mínimo con perros domésticos.
es.actions: Apoya las campañas de vacunación del Programa de Conservación del Lobo Etíope, respalda las zonas de exclusión de ganado en el hábitat principal del lobo y financia el monitoreo de brotes de enfermedades.

pt.description: O lobo-etíope é o carnívoro mais ameaçado de África e o canídeo mais raro do mundo, restrito às terras altas afroalpinas da Etiópia onde caça roedores afroalpinos.
pt.endangerment: A raiva e a cinomose canina se espalhando de cães domésticos causaram epidemias devastadoras. A invasão agrícola está reduzindo seu habitat de grande altitude.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagens afroalpinas abertas acima de 3.000 metros nas terras altas etíopes, especialmente as Montanhas Bale, com abundantes presas de roedores e contato mínimo com cães domésticos.
pt.actions: Apoie as campanhas de vacinação do Programa de Conservação do Lobo-etíope, respalda as zonas de exclusão de gado no habitat principal do lobo e finance o monitoramento de surtos de doenças.

fr.description: Le loup d'Éthiopie est le carnivore le plus menacé d'Afrique et le canidé le plus rare du monde, cantonné aux hautes terres afroalpines d'Éthiopie où il chasse les rongeurs afroalpins.
fr.endangerment: La rage et la maladie de Carré se propageant depuis les chiens domestiques ont causé des épidémies dévastatrices. L'empiètement agricole réduit son habitat de haute altitude.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des prairies afroalpines ouvertes au-dessus de 3 000 mètres dans les hautes terres éthiopiennes, notamment les Monts Bale, avec d'abondantes proies de rongeurs et un contact minimal avec les chiens domestiques.
fr.actions: Soutenez les campagnes de vaccination du Programme de conservation du loup d'Éthiopie, appuyez les zones d'exclusion du bétail dans l'habitat principal du loup et financez la surveillance des épidémies.

ja.endangerment: Rabies and canine distemper spreading from 野生 dogs have caused devastating epidemics. Agricultural encroachment is shrinking its high-altitude habitat.

en.description: The Tonkin snub-nosed monkey is a striking primate with an upturned nose, found only in a few fragmented forest patches in northern Vietnam, with fewer than 250 individuals estimated in the wild.
en.endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and deforestation for shifting cultivation have reduced this species to critically small numbers.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Limestone karst forest and tropical montane forest in northern Vietnam, particularly in Na Hang and Khau Ca nature reserves, with seasonal fruit and leaf availability.
en.actions: Support Vietnam's Tonkin snub-nosed monkey sanctuaries, fund community conservation around Khau Ca, and back strict hunting prohibition enforcement.

es.description: El mono de nariz chata de Tonkín es un llamativo primate con una nariz respingona, encontrado solo en unos pocos parches de bosque fragmentados en el norte de Vietnam, con menos de 250 individuos estimados en la naturaleza.
es.endangerment: La caza para carne, la medicina tradicional y la deforestación para la agricultura itinerante han reducido esta especie a números críticamente pequeños.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque kárstico de piedra caliza y bosque tropical montano en el norte de Vietnam, particularmente en las reservas naturales de Na Hang y Khau Ca, con disponibilidad estacional de frutos y hojas.
es.actions: Apoya los santuarios del mono de nariz chata de Tonkín de Vietnam, financia la conservación comunitaria alrededor de Khau Ca y respalda la aplicación estricta de la prohibición de caza.

pt.description: O macaco-narigudo-de-tonquim é um primata marcante com nariz arrebitado, encontrado apenas em alguns fragmentos de floresta no norte do Vietnã, com menos de 250 indivíduos estimados na natureza.
pt.endangerment: A caça para carne, a medicina tradicional e o desmatamento para a agricultura itinerante reduziram esta espécie a números criticamente pequenos.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta cárstica de calcário e floresta tropical montana no norte do Vietnã, particularmente nas reservas naturais de Na Hang e Khau Ca, com disponibilidade sazonal de frutos e folhas.
pt.actions: Apoie os santuários do macaco-narigudo-de-tonquim do Vietnã, finance a conservação comunitária ao redor de Khau Ca e respalda a aplicação estrita da proibição de caça.

fr.description: Le rhinopithèque du Tonkin est un primate frappant au nez retroussé, trouvé uniquement dans quelques fragments de forêt dans le nord du Vietnam, avec moins de 250 individus estimés à l'état sauvage.
fr.endangerment: La chasse pour la viande, la médecine traditionnelle et la déforestation pour l'agriculture itinérante ont réduit cette espèce à des effectifs critiquement faibles.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt karstique calcaire et forêt tropicale montagnarde dans le nord du Vietnam, notamment dans les réserves naturelles de Na Hang et Khau Ca, avec une disponibilité saisonnière de fruits et de feuilles.
fr.actions: Soutenez les sanctuaires du rhinopithèque du Tonkin au Vietnam, financez la conservation communautaire autour de Khau Ca et appuyez l'application stricte de l'interdiction de chasse.

ja.endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and 森林伐採 for shifting cultivation have 削減d this 種 to 極めて危急 小さい numbers.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Limestone karst 森林 and 熱帯 montane 森林 in northern Vietnam, particularly in Na Hang and Khau Ca nature reserves, with 季節 fruit and leaf availability.
ja.actions: Support Vietnam's Tonkin snub-nosed monkey sanctuaries, fund コミュニティ 保全 around Khau Ca, and back strict 狩猟 prohibition enforcement.

ar.endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and إزالة الغابات for shifting cultivation have يقللd this الأنواع to حرج صغير numbers.

ar.actions: Support Vietnam's Tonkin snub-nosed monkey sanctuaries, fund مجتمع الحفظ around Khau Ca, and back strict الصيد prohibition enforcement.

hi.description: The Tonkin snub-nosed monkey is a striking primate with an upturned nose, found only in a few fragmented वन patches in northern Vietnam, with कम से कम 250 व्यक्ति अनुमानित in the वन्य.
hi.endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and वनों की कटाई for shifting cultivation have कमd this प्रजातियाँ to गंभीर रूप से छोटा numbers.

en.description: The riverine rabbit is one of Africa's most endangered mammals, restricted to the dense riverine scrub along seasonal rivers in South Africa's Karoo semi-desert.
en.endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated cropland has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall riverine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of seasonal rivers in the central Karoo, South Africa, for shelter and breeding.
en.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's riverine rabbit programme, fund riparian habitat restoration on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in conservation stewardship.

es.description: El conejo ribereño es uno de los mamíferos más amenazados de África, restringido al denso matorral ribereño a lo largo de ríos estacionales en el Karoo semidesértico de Sudáfrica.
es.endangerment: La conversión del arbusto ribereño en tierras de cultivo irrigadas ha destruido el hábitat especializado del que depende completamente este conejo, dejando pequeñas poblaciones fragmentadas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Vegetación ribereña densa y alta —particularmente arbustos del Karoo— a lo largo de las orillas de los ríos estacionales en el Karoo central, Sudáfrica, para refugio y reproducción.
es.actions: Apoya el programa de conejo ribereño del Endangered Wildlife Trust, financia la restauración del hábitat ribereño en granjas privadas y atrae a los propietarios de tierras del Karoo hacia la corresponsabilidad de conservación.

pt.description: O coelho-ribeirinho é um dos mamíferos mais ameaçados da África, restrito ao denso arbusto ribeirinho ao longo dos rios sazonais no Karoo semidesértico da África do Sul.
pt.endangerment: A conversão do arbusto ribeirinho em terras de cultivo irrigadas destruiu o habitat especializado do qual este coelho depende completamente, deixando populações fragmentadas minúsculas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Vegetação ribeirinha densa e alta — particularmente arbustos do Karoo — ao longo das margens dos rios sazonais no Karoo central, África do Sul, para abrigo e reprodução.
pt.actions: Apoie o programa de coelho-ribeirinho do Endangered Wildlife Trust, finance a restauração do habitat ribeirinho em fazendas privadas e envolva os proprietários de terras do Karoo na corresponsabilidade de conservação.

fr.description: Le lapin des rivières est l'un des mammifères les plus menacés d'Afrique, cantonné aux épaisses broussailles riveraines le long des rivières saisonnières dans le semi-désert de Karoo en Afrique du Sud.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des broussailles riveraines en terres cultivées irriguées a détruit l'habitat spécialisé dont ce lapin dépend entièrement, laissant de minuscules populations fragmentées.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une végétation riveraine dense et haute — notamment les broussailles du Karoo — le long des berges des rivières saisonnières dans le Karoo central, en Afrique du Sud, pour l'abri et la reproduction.
fr.actions: Soutenez le programme du lapin des rivières de l'Endangered Wildlife Trust, financez la restauration de l'habitat riverain sur les terres privées et engagez les propriétaires terriens du Karoo dans des accords de gestion pour la conservation.

ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall 川ine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of 季節 川s in the central Karoo, South アフリカ, for shelter and breeding.
ja.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's 川ine rabbit プログラムme, fund riparian habitat 修復 on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in 保全 stewardship.

zh.endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated cro计划d has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented 种群s.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall 河流ine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of 季节性 河流s in the central Karoo, South 非洲, for shelter and breeding.
zh.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's 河流ine rabbit 项目me, fund riparian habitat 恢复 on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in 保护 stewardship.

ar.description: The نهرine rabbit is one of أفريقيا's most مهدد ثدييات, restricted to the dense نهرine scrub along موسمي نهرs in South أفريقيا's Karoo semi-desert.
ar.endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated croخطةd has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented عدد السكانs.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall نهرine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of موسمي نهرs in the central Karoo, South أفريقيا, for shelter and breeding.
ar.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's نهرine rabbit برنامجme, fund riparian habitat إعادة on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in الحفظ stewardship.

hi.description: The नदीine rabbit is one of अफ्रीका's most लुप्तप्राय स्तनधारी, restricted to the dense नदीine scrub along मौसमी नदीs in South अफ्रीका's Karoo semi-desert.
hi.endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated croयोजनाd has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented जनसंख्याs.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall नदीine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of मौसमी नदीs in the central Karoo, South अफ्रीका, for shelter and breeding.
hi.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's नदीine rabbit कार्यक्रमme, fund riparian habitat पुनर्स्थापना on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in संरक्षण stewardship.

en.description: The Cuban solenodon is a small, venomous insectivore that is one of the most ancient surviving mammals in the Caribbean, resembling early Mesozoic insectivores from 76 million years ago.
en.endangerment: Introduced mongoose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. Deforestation further shrinks its forest refuge.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact humid forest with deep leaf litter and soil for burrowing and foraging in eastern Cuba, particularly in Alejandro de Humboldt National Park.
en.actions: Support invasive predator control in key Cuban forest reserves, fund camera trap surveys to determine remaining population size, and back Cuba's national parks management.

es.description: El solenodon cubano es un pequeño insectívoro venenoso que es uno de los mamíferos supervivientes más antiguos del Caribe, parecido a los insectívoros del Mesozoico de hace 76 millones de años.
es.endangerment: Las mangostas y los gatos introducidos depredan a este mamífero terrestre que evolucionó sin depredadores mamíferos. La deforestación reduce aún más su refugio forestal.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque húmedo intacto con hojarasca profunda y suelo para excavar y forrajear en el este de Cuba, particularmente en el Parque Nacional Alejandro de Humboldt.
es.actions: Apoya el control de depredadores invasores en las principales reservas forestales cubanas, financia estudios con cámaras trampa para determinar el tamaño de la población restante y respalda la gestión de los parques nacionales de Cuba.

pt.description: O solenodonte-cubano é um pequeno insectívoro venenoso que é um dos mamíferos sobreviventes mais antigos do Caribe, parecido com insectívoros mesozóicos de 76 milhões de anos atrás.
pt.endangerment: Mangustos e gatos introduzidos predam este mamífero terrestre que evoluiu sem predadores mamíferos. O desmatamento reduz ainda mais seu refúgio florestal.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta úmida intacta com folhagem profunda e solo para escavar e forragear no leste de Cuba, particularmente no Parque Nacional Alejandro de Humboldt.
pt.actions: Apoie o controle de predadores invasores nas principais reservas florestais cubanas, finance pesquisas com armadilhas fotográficas para determinar o tamanho da população restante e respalda a gestão dos parques nacionais de Cuba.

fr.description: Le solénodon de Cuba est un petit insectivore venimeux et l'un des mammifères survivants les plus anciens des Caraïbes, ressemblant aux insectivores mésozoïques vieux de 76 millions d'années.
fr.endangerment: Les mangoustes et les chats introduits prédatent ce mammifère terrestre qui a évolué sans prédateurs mammifères. La déforestation réduit encore davantage son refuge forestier.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt humide intacte avec une litière profonde et un sol pour fouiller et fourrager dans l'est de Cuba, notamment dans le Parc national Alejandro de Humboldt.
fr.actions: Soutenez le contrôle des prédateurs envahissants dans les principales réserves forestières cubaines, financez des enquêtes par caméras-pièges pour déterminer la taille de la population restante et appuyez la gestion des parcs nationaux de Cuba.

zh.description: The Cuban solenodon is a 小型, venomous insectivore that is one of the most 古代 surviving 哺乳动物 in the 加勒比地区, resembling early Mesozoic insectivores from 76 百万 years ago.
zh.endangerment: Introduced mo非政府组织ose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. De森林ation further shrinks its 森林 refuge.

ar.endangerment: Introduced moمنظمة غير حكوميةose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. Deغابةation further shrinks its غابة refuge.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Intact رطب غابة with deep leaf litter and soil for burrowing and foraging in eastern Cuba, particularly in Alejandro de Humboldt National Park.

hi.description: The Cuban solenodon is a छोटा, venomous insectivore that is one of the most प्राचीन surviving स्तनधारी in the कैरिबियन, resembling early Mesozoic insectivores from 76 मिलियन years ago.
hi.endangerment: Introduced moएनजीओose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. Deवनation further shrinks its वन refuge.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Intact आर्द्र वन with deep leaf litter and soil for burrowing and foraging in eastern Cuba, particularly in Alejandro de Humboldt National Park.

en.description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two small Japanese islands — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense subtropical forest and is considered a living fossil.
en.endangerment: Introduced mongooses, cats, and dogs prey heavily on this ground-dwelling rabbit; deforestation for forestry and development further reduces available habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense subtropical forest with a closed canopy, leaf litter for foraging, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima islands, Japan.
en.actions: Support ongoing mongoose eradication on Amami-Oshima, fund feral cat and dog control programmes, and back forest protection on both islands.

es.description: El conejo de Amami es un conejo primitivo y de pelaje oscuro restringido a dos pequeñas islas japonesas —Amami-Oshima y Tokunoshima— donde vive en denso bosque subtropical y es considerado un fósil viviente.
es.endangerment: Las mangostas, los gatos y los perros introducidos depredan fuertemente a este conejo terrestre; la deforestación para la silvicultura y el desarrollo reduce aún más el hábitat disponible.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque subtropical denso con dosel cerrado, hojarasca para forrajear y lugares de madriguera seguros en las islas Amami-Oshima y Tokunoshima, Japón.
es.actions: Apoya la erradicación continua de mangostas en Amami-Oshima, financia programas de control de gatos y perros salvajes y respalda la protección forestal en ambas islas.

pt.description: O coelho-de-amami é um coelho primitivo e de pelagem escura restrito a duas pequenas ilhas japonesas — Amami-Oshima e Tokunoshima — onde vive em densa floresta subtropical e é considerado um fóssil vivo.
pt.endangerment: Mangustos, gatos e cães introduzidos predam intensamente este coelho terrestre; o desmatamento para silvicultura e desenvolvimento reduz ainda mais o habitat disponível.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta subtropical densa com dossel fechado, folhagem para forrageamento e locais de toca seguros nas ilhas Amami-Oshima e Tokunoshima, Japão.
pt.actions: Apoie a erradicação contínua de mangustos em Amami-Oshima, finance programas de controle de gatos e cães selvagens e respalda a proteção florestal em ambas as ilhas.

fr.description: Le lapin d'Amami est un lapin primitif à la fourrure sombre cantonné à deux petites îles japonaises — Amami-Oshima et Tokunoshima — où il vit dans une forêt subtropicale dense et est considéré comme un fossile vivant.
fr.endangerment: Les mangoustes, chats et chiens introduits prédatent fortement ce lapin terrestre ; la déforestation pour la sylviculture et le développement réduit encore davantage l'habitat disponible.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt subtropicale dense à canopée fermée, avec une litière pour le fourrage et des sites de terrier sûrs sur les îles d'Amami-Oshima et de Tokunoshima, au Japon.
fr.actions: Soutenez l'éradication continue des mangoustes à Amami-Oshima, financez des programmes de contrôle des chats et chiens sauvages et appuyez la protection forestière sur les deux îles.

ja.description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two 小さい Japanese 島s — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense sub熱帯 森林 and is considered a living fossil.
ja.endangerment: Introduced moNGOoses, cats, and dogs prey heavily on this ground-dwelling rabbit; 森林伐採 for 森林ry and 開発 further 削減s 利用可 habitat.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dense sub熱帯 森林 with a closed canopy, leaf litter for 採取, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima 島s, Japan.
ja.actions: Support oNGOing moNGOose eradication on Amami-Oshima, fund feral cat and dog control プログラムmes, and back 森林 保護ion on both 島s.

ar.description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two صغير Japanese جزيرةs — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense subاستوائي غابة and is considered a living fossil.
ar.endangerment: Introduced moمنظمة غير حكوميةoses, cats, and dogs prey heavily on this ground-dwelling rabbit; إزالة الغابات for غابةry and التطوير further يقللs available habitat.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Dense subاستوائي غابة with a closed canopy, leaf litter for foraging, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima جزيرةs, Japan.

hi.description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two छोटा Japanese द्वीपs — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense subउष्णकटिबंधीय वन and is considered a living fossil.

hi.ecosystemNeeds: Dense subउष्णकटिबंधीय वन with a closed canopy, leaf litter for foraging, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima द्वीपs, Japan.
hi.actions: Support oएनजीओing moएनजीओose eradication on Amami-Oshima, fund feral cat and dog control कार्यक्रमmes, and back वन सुरक्षाion on both द्वीपs.
