en.description: The Alabama cavefish is the rarest cave fish in North America, known only from a single cave pool in Key Cave, Alabama. This completely blind, depigmented fish has never been seen in any other location.
en.endangerment: The species depends entirely on bat guano from cave-roosting grey bats as its base food source; declines in bat populations from white-nose syndrome are reducing the food supply, while agricultural pesticide runoff pollutes the cave pool.
en.ecosystemNeeds: A single cave pool in Key Cave National Wildlife Refuge, Alabama, fed by subterranean water and sustained by nutrients from grey bat guano; requires absolute water quality, stable hydrology, and bat colony health.
en.actions: Support USFWS Key Cave NWR management, fund white-nose syndrome research for cave-roosting grey bats, and advocate for strict agricultural buffer zones around the cave drainage watershed.

es.description: El pez cavernícola de Alabama es el pez cavernícola más raro de América del Norte, conocido solo en una única charca subterránea en Key Cave, Alabama. Este pez completamente ciego y despigmentado nunca ha sido visto en ningún otro lugar.
es.endangerment: La especie depende enteramente del guano de murciélago de los murciélagos grises que viven en la cueva como su fuente de alimento base; los declives en las poblaciones de murciélagos por el síndrome de la nariz blanca están reduciendo el suministro de alimento, mientras que la escorrentía de pesticidas agrícolas contamina la charca subterránea.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Una única charca subterránea en el Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentada por agua subterránea y sostenida por nutrientes del guano de murciélago gris; requiere calidad absoluta del agua, hidrología estable y salud de la colonia de murciélagos.
es.actions: Apoyar la gestión del USFWS Key Cave NWR, financiar investigaciones sobre el síndrome de la nariz blanca para los murciélagos grises que viven en cuevas y abogar por estrictas zonas de amortiguación agrícola alrededor de la cuenca de drenaje de la cueva.

pt.description: O peixe-cavernícola-do-alabama é o peixe cavernícola mais raro da América do Norte, conhecido apenas em uma única poça subterrânea na Caverna Key, Alabama. Este peixe completamente cego e despigmentado nunca foi visto em nenhum outro local.
pt.endangerment: A espécie depende inteiramente do guano de morcego dos morcegos cinzas que habitam a caverna como sua fonte de alimento base; os declínios nas populações de morcegos pelo síndrome do nariz branco estão reduzindo o suprimento de alimento, enquanto o escoamento de pesticidas agrícolas contamina a poça subterrânea.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Uma única poça subterrânea no Refúgio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentada por água subterrânea e sustentada por nutrientes do guano de morcego cinza; requer qualidade absoluta da água, hidrologia estável e saúde da colônia de morcegos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo do USFWS Key Cave NWR, financiar pesquisas sobre o síndrome do nariz branco para morcegos cinzas de cavernas e defender zonas de amortecimento agrícola rigorosas ao redor da bacia de drenagem da caverna.

fr.description: Le poisson des cavernes de l'Alabama est le poisson cavernicole le plus rare d'Amérique du Nord, connu uniquement dans une seule mare souterraine dans Key Cave, Alabama. Ce poisson complètement aveugle et dépigmenté n'a jamais été observé dans aucun autre endroit.
fr.endangerment: L'espèce dépend entièrement du guano des chauves-souris grises vivant dans la grotte comme base alimentaire ; les déclins des populations de chauves-souris dus au syndrome du museau blanc réduisent l'approvisionnement alimentaire, tandis que le ruissellement de pesticides agricoles pollue la mare souterraine.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une seule mare souterraine dans le refuge national de faune sauvage de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentée par des eaux souterraines et soutenue par les nutriments du guano des chauves-souris grises ; nécessite une qualité d'eau absolue, une hydrologie stable et la santé de la colonie de chauves-souris.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion du USFWS Key Cave NWR, financer la recherche sur le syndrome du museau blanc pour les chauves-souris grises cavernicoles et plaider pour des zones tampons agricoles strictes autour du bassin de drainage de la grotte.

ja.description: The Alabama cave魚 is the まれst cave 魚 in 北アメリカ, 知るn only from a single cave pool in Key 洞窟, Alabama. This completely blind, depigmented 魚 has never been seen in any other location.

ja.ecosystemNeeds: A single cave pool in Key 洞窟 National Wildlife Refuge, Alabama, fed by subterranean water and sustained by nutrients from grey bat guano; requires absolute water quality, 安定 hydrology, and bat colony health.

en.description: The Mangarahara cichlid is a freshwater fish from a small river system in northeastern Madagascar. The Mangarahara River is believed to have dried up completely, and the species was declared extinct in the wild around 2013.
en.endangerment: The Mangarahara River in Mahajanga province has been dammed for irrigation and extraction, causing the river to dry up seasonally; the last wild individuals disappeared when the river failed entirely.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The Mangarahara River and its tributaries in northeastern Madagascar, with clear, flowing water, sandy and rocky substrate, and riverine gallery forest providing shade and organic input.
en.actions: Support ZSL London Zoo and Bristol Zoo's Mangarahara cichlid captive breeding programme, fund hydrological surveys to identify any remaining wild habitat, and advocate for river flow restoration in Mahajanga.

es.description: El cíclido Mangarahara es un pez de agua dulce de un pequeño sistema fluvial en el noreste de Madagascar. Se cree que el Río Mangarahara se ha secado completamente, y la especie fue declarada extinta en la naturaleza alrededor de 2013.
es.endangerment: El Río Mangarahara en la provincia de Mahajanga ha sido represado para irrigación y extracción, provocando que el río se seque estacionalmente; los últimos individuos silvestres desaparecieron cuando el río falló completamente.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El Río Mangarahara y sus afluentes en el noreste de Madagascar, con agua clara y fluyente, sustrato arenoso y rocoso, y bosque galería ribereño que proporciona sombra y aporte orgánico.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio del cíclido Mangarahara del ZSL London Zoo y Bristol Zoo, financiar encuestas hidrológicas para identificar cualquier hábitat silvestre restante y abogar por la restauración del flujo fluvial en Mahajanga.

pt.description: O cíclido-mangarahara é um peixe de água doce de um pequeno sistema fluvial no nordeste de Madagascar. Acredita-se que o Rio Mangarahara tenha secado completamente, e a espécie foi declarada extinta na natureza por volta de 2013.
pt.endangerment: O Rio Mangarahara na província de Mahajanga foi represado para irrigação e extração, fazendo com que o rio secasse sazonalmente; os últimos indivíduos selvagens desapareceram quando o rio falhou completamente.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O Rio Mangarahara e seus afluentes no nordeste de Madagascar, com água clara e fluente, substrato arenoso e rochoso, e floresta galeria ribeirinha fornecendo sombra e aporte orgânico.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do cíclido Mangarahara do ZSL London Zoo e Bristol Zoo, financiar levantamentos hidrológicos para identificar qualquer habitat selvagem restante e defender a restauração do fluxo fluvial em Mahajanga.

fr.description: Le cichlidé de Mangarahara est un poisson d'eau douce d'un petit système fluvial dans le nord-est de Madagascar. La rivière Mangarahara est réputée avoir complètement tari, et l'espèce a été déclarée éteinte à l'état sauvage vers 2013.
fr.endangerment: La rivière Mangarahara dans la province de Mahajanga a été endiguée pour l'irrigation et l'extraction, entraînant l'assèchement saisonnier de la rivière ; les derniers individus sauvages ont disparu lorsque la rivière a complètement tari.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: La rivière Mangarahara et ses affluents dans le nord-est de Madagascar, avec de l'eau claire et courante, un substrat sableux et rocheux, et une forêt galerie riveraine fournissant de l'ombre et des apports organiques.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité du cichlidé de Mangarahara du ZSL London Zoo et du Bristol Zoo, financer des enquêtes hydrologiques pour identifier tout habitat sauvage restant et plaider pour la restauration du débit fluvial à Mahajanga.

en.description: The Clanwilliam cedar is a majestic conifer that once covered large swathes of the Cederberg mountains in the Western Cape. Now reduced to scattered, mostly elderly individuals, the species is failing to regenerate in a warming, fire-prone landscape.
en.endangerment: More frequent and intense wildfires, driven by climate change and invasive pine that fuels hotter fires, kill adult trees before seeds can mature; surviving trees are mainly old individuals with poor regeneration rates.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Rocky sandstone plateaux and cliff faces in the Cederberg Wilderness Area at 900–1,500 metres elevation, in the protea-dominated mountain fynbos, with fire-return intervals of at least 15 years for successful regeneration.
en.actions: Support Cape Nature's Cederberg cedar restoration programme, fund active replanting in fire-managed areas, and advocate for invasive pine clearance throughout the Cederberg to reduce catastrophic fire intensity.

es.description: El cedro de Clanwilliam es un majestuoso conífera que alguna vez cubrió grandes franjas de las montañas Cederberg en el Cabo Occidental. Ahora reducido a individuos dispersos, en su mayoría ancianos, la especie no está logrando regenerarse en un paisaje que se calienta y es propenso a incendios.
es.endangerment: Los incendios forestales más frecuentes e intensos, impulsados por el cambio climático y el pino invasor que alimenta incendios más calientes, matan a los árboles adultos antes de que las semillas puedan madurar; los árboles supervivientes son principalmente individuos viejos con tasas de regeneración deficientes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Mesetas de arenisca rocosa y acantilados en la Área Silvestre de Cederberg a 900-1.500 metros de altitud, en el fynbos montañoso dominado por proteas, con intervalos de retorno de incendios de al menos 15 años para una regeneración exitosa.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de restauración del cedro Cederberg de Cape Nature, financiar la replantación activa en áreas gestionadas contra incendios y abogar por la eliminación de pinos invasores en todo el Cederberg para reducir la intensidad de incendios catastróficos.

pt.description: O cedro-de-clanwilliam é uma majestosa conífera que antes cobria grandes extensões das montanhas Cederberg no Cabo Ocidental. Agora reduzido a indivíduos dispersos, principalmente idosos, a espécie não está conseguindo se regenerar em uma paisagem que aquece e é propensa a incêndios.
pt.endangerment: Incêndios florestais mais frequentes e intensos, impulsionados pelas mudanças climáticas e pelo pinheiro invasor que alimenta incêndios mais quentes, matam as árvores adultas antes que as sementes possam amadurecer; as árvores sobreviventes são principalmente indivíduos velhos com baixas taxas de regeneração.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planaltos de arenito rochoso e falésias na Área Silvestre de Cederberg a 900-1.500 metros de altitude, no fynbos montanhoso dominado por proteas, com intervalos de retorno de incêndio de pelo menos 15 anos para regeneração bem-sucedida.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de restauração do cedro-de-cederberg da Cape Nature, financiar o replantio ativo em áreas gerenciadas contra incêndios e defender a limpeza de pinheiros invasores em todo o Cederberg para reduzir a intensidade de incêndios catastróficos.

fr.description: Le cèdre de Clanwilliam est un conifère majestueux qui couvrait autrefois de larges étendues des montagnes du Cederberg dans le Cap-Occidental. Désormais réduit à des individus épars, principalement âgés, l'espèce ne parvient pas à se régénérer dans un paysage se réchauffant et sujet aux incendies.
fr.endangerment: Des incendies de forêt plus fréquents et intenses, alimentés par le changement climatique et les pins envahissants qui nourrissent des incendies plus chauds, tuent les arbres adultes avant que les graines puissent mûrir ; les arbres survivants sont principalement de vieux individus avec de faibles taux de régénération.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Plateaux de grès rocheux et falaises dans la zone sauvage du Cederberg à 900-1 500 mètres d'altitude, dans le fynbos montagnard dominé par les protéas, avec des intervalles de retour du feu d'au moins 15 ans pour une régénération réussie.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de restauration du cèdre du Cederberg de Cape Nature, financer la replantation active dans les zones gérées contre les incendies et plaider pour l'élimination des pins envahissants dans tout le Cederberg pour réduire l'intensité des incendies catastrophiques.

en.description: The St. Helena ebony is a small flowering tree endemic to the remote island of St. Helena in the South Atlantic. Once extinct in the wild, it was rescued from total extinction by cuttings grown in cultivation and subsequently replanted on the island.
en.endangerment: Introduced goats eliminated all native St. Helena forest over centuries; by 1998, the species was considered extinct in the wild with only a handful of cultivated individuals remaining.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Semi-arid rocky slopes in the central cloud forest zone of St. Helena island at 500–800 metres, with cloud moisture, sheltered aspects, and goat-free conditions that allow natural regeneration.
en.actions: Support the St. Helena National Trust's ebony reintroduction programme, fund goat exclusion fencing around planting sites, and advocate for native forest restoration across the island's central ridge.

es.description: El ébano de Santa Elena es un pequeño árbol con flores endémico de la remota isla de Santa Elena en el Atlántico Sur. Antes extinto en la naturaleza, fue rescatado de la extinción total por esquejes cultivados en cautiverio y posteriormente replantado en la isla.
es.endangerment: Las cabras introducidas eliminaron todo el bosque nativo de Santa Elena durante siglos; para 1998, la especie se consideraba extinta en la naturaleza con solo un puñado de individuos cultivados restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Laderas rocosas semiáridas en la zona del bosque nuboso central de Santa Elena a 500-800 metros, con humedad de nubes, aspectos resguardados y condiciones libres de cabras que permitan la regeneración natural.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de reintroducción del ébano del Fideicomiso Nacional de Santa Elena, financiar cercas de exclusión de cabras alrededor de los sitios de plantación y abogar por la restauración del bosque nativo en toda la cresta central de la isla.

pt.description: O ébano-de-santa-helena é uma pequena árvore florida endêmica da remota ilha de Santa Helena no Atlântico Sul. Antes extinto na natureza, foi resgatado da extinção total por estacas cultivadas em cativeiro e posteriormente replantadas na ilha.
pt.endangerment: Cabras introduzidas eliminaram toda a floresta nativa de Santa Helena ao longo dos séculos; em 1998, a espécie era considerada extinta na natureza com apenas um punhado de indivíduos cultivados restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Encostas rochosas semiáridas na zona da floresta nebular central de Santa Helena a 500-800 metros, com umidade de nuvens, aspectos abrigados e condições livres de cabras que permitem a regeneração natural.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de reintrodução do ébano do Fideicomisso Nacional de Santa Helena, financiar cercas de exclusão de cabras ao redor dos locais de plantio e defender a restauração da floresta nativa em toda a crista central da ilha.

fr.description: L'ébène de Sainte-Hélène est un petit arbre à fleurs endémique de la lointaine île de Sainte-Hélène dans l'Atlantique Sud. Autrefois éteint à l'état sauvage, il a été sauvé de l'extinction totale par des boutures cultivées en captivité puis replantées sur l'île.
fr.endangerment: Les chèvres introduites ont éliminé toute la forêt indigène de Sainte-Hélène au fil des siècles ; en 1998, l'espèce était considérée comme éteinte à l'état sauvage avec seulement une poignée d'individus cultivés restants.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Versants rocheux semi-arides dans la zone de forêt nuageuse centrale de Sainte-Hélène à 500-800 mètres, avec humidité nuageuse, versants abrités et conditions sans chèvres permettant la régénération naturelle.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de réintroduction de l'ébène du National Trust de Sainte-Hélène, financer des clôtures d'exclusion des chèvres autour des sites de plantation et plaider pour la restauration des forêts indigènes sur toute la crête centrale de l'île.

hi.description: The St. Helena ebony is a छोटा flowering tree स्थानिक to the remote द्वीप of St. Helena in the South Atlantic. Once extinct in the वन्य, it was rescued from total extinction by cuttings grown in cultivation and subsequently reयोजनाted on the द्वीप.
hi.endangerment: Introduced goats eliminated all native St. Helena वन over centuries; by 1998, the प्रजातियाँ was considered extinct in the वन्य with only a handful of cultivated व्यक्ति remaining.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Semi-शुष्क rocky slopes in the central cloud वन zone of St. Helena द्वीप at 500–800 metres, with cloud moisture, sheltered aspects, and goat-free conditions that allow natural regeneration.
hi.actions: Support the St. Helena National Trust's ebony पुनर्प्रवर्तन कार्यक्रमme, fund goat exclusion fencing around योजनाting sites, and समर्थन for native वन पुनर्स्थापना across the द्वीप's central ridge.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: Wallace's giant bee is the world's largest bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian islands in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its rediscovery in 2019.
en.endangerment: Deforestation for cocoa, oil palm, and logging has destroyed most of the lowland forest habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera islands; the species' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland tropical forest on the North Maluku islands of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding forest producing resin.
en.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku programme to protect lowland forest, advocate for CITES enforcement against wildlife collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining populations.

es.description: La abeja gigante de Wallace es la abeja más grande del mundo, con una envergadura de 6 cm y grandes mandíbulas usadas para recolectar resina de árboles. Conocida solo en algunas islas indonesias en el Norte de Maluku, estuvo perdida para la ciencia durante 38 años hasta su redescubrimiento en 2019.
es.endangerment: La deforestación para cacao, palma de aceite y tala ha destruido la mayor parte del hábitat de bosque de tierras bajas en las islas de Bacan, Obi y Halmahera; los sitios de anidamiento de la especie en termiteros ocupados son destruidos con los árboles que habitan.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical de tierras bajas en las islas del Norte de Maluku de Indonesia, particularmente Bacan y Obi, con nidos de termitas arbóreas en árboles altos que la abeja usa como sitios de anidamiento y el bosque circundante que produce resina.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa del Norte de Maluku de la Wildlife Conservation Society para proteger el bosque de tierras bajas, abogar por la aplicación de la CITES contra los coleccionistas de vida silvestre y financiar encuestas sistemáticas de sitios de nidificación para mapear las poblaciones restantes.

pt.description: A abelha-gigante-de-wallace é a maior abelha do mundo, com envergadura de 6 cm e grandes mandíbulas usadas para coletar resina de árvores. Conhecida apenas em algumas ilhas indonésias no Norte de Maluku, estava perdida para a ciência por 38 anos até sua redescoberta em 2019.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para cacau, óleo de palma e extração de madeira destruiu a maior parte do habitat de floresta de baixadas nas ilhas de Bacan, Obi e Halmahera; os locais de nidificação da espécie em ninhos de cupins habitados são destruídos com as árvores que habitam.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de baixadas nas ilhas do Norte de Maluku da Indonésia, particularmente Bacan e Obi, com ninhos de cupins arborícolas em árvores altas que a abelha usa como locais de nidificação e a floresta circundante produzindo resina.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa do Norte de Maluku da Wildlife Conservation Society para proteger a floresta de baixadas, defender a aplicação da CITES contra os coletores de vida silvestre e financiar levantamentos sistemáticos de locais de nidificação para mapear as populações restantes.

fr.description: L'abeille géante de Wallace est la plus grande abeille du monde, avec une envergure de 6 cm et de grandes mandibules utilisées pour récolter la résine des arbres. Connue seulement de quelques îles indonésiennes dans le Nord des Moluques, elle était perdue pour la science pendant 38 ans jusqu'à sa redécouverte en 2019.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation pour le cacao, l'huile de palme et l'exploitation forestière a détruit la majeure partie de l'habitat forestier de basse altitude sur les îles de Bacan, Obi et Halmahera ; les sites de nidification de l'espèce dans des termitières occupées sont détruits avec les arbres qu'ils habitent.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale de basse altitude sur les îles du Nord des Moluques d'Indonésie, notamment Bacan et Obi, avec des nids de termites arboricoles dans de grands arbres que l'abeille utilise comme sites de nidification et la forêt environnante produisant de la résine.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme Nord Moluques de la Wildlife Conservation Society pour protéger la forêt de basse altitude, plaider pour l'application de la CITES contre les collectionneurs de faune sauvage et financer des enquêtes systématiques des sites de nidification pour cartographier les populations restantes.

ja.endangerment: De森林ation for cocoa, oil palm, and 伐採 has 破壊 most of the lowland 森林 habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera 島s; the 種' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are 破壊 with the trees they inhabit.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland 熱帯 森林 on the North Maluku 島s of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with 樹上 termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding 森林 producing resin.
ja.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku プログラムme to 保護 lowland 森林, 提唱 for CITES enforcement against 野生生物 collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining 個体数s.

zh.description: Wallace's giant bee is the 世界's 最大 bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian 岛屿s in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its re发现y in 2019.
zh.endangerment: De森林ation for cocoa, oil palm, and 伐木 has destroyed most of the lowland 森林 habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera 岛屿s; the 物种' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland 热带 森林 on the North Maluku 岛屿s of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding 森林 producing resin.
zh.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku 项目me to 保护 lowland 森林, 倡导 for CITES enforcement against 野生动物 collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining 种群s.

ar.description: Wallace's giant bee is the العالم's أكبر bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian جزيرةs in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its reيكتشفy in 2019.
ar.endangerment: Deغابةation for cocoa, oil palm, and قطع الأشجار has destroyed most of the lowland غابة habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera جزيرةs; the الأنواع' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland استوائي غابة on the North Maluku جزيرةs of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding غابة producing resin.
ar.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku برنامجme to يحمي lowland غابة, يدافع for CITES enforcement against الحياة البرية collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining عدد السكانs.

hi.description: Wallace's giant bee is the विश्व's सबसे बड़ा bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian द्वीपs in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its reखोजेंy in 2019.
hi.endangerment: Deवनation for cocoa, oil palm, and लकड़ी काटना has destroyed most of the lowland वन habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera द्वीपs; the प्रजातियाँ' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland उष्णकटिबंधीय वन on the North Maluku द्वीपs of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding वन producing resin.
hi.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku कार्यक्रमme to सुरक्षा lowland वन, समर्थन for CITES enforcement against वन्यजीव collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining जनसंख्याs.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The monarch butterfly undertakes one of nature's most extraordinary migrations, travelling up to 4,800 km from Canada and the USA to overwintering colonies in the oyamel fir forests of central Mexico. The migratory population was added to the IUCN Red List as Critically Endangered in 2022.
en.endangerment: Loss of milkweed through herbicide use and agriculture in North America has removed the only plant monarch caterpillars can eat; climate extremes disrupt migration; illegal logging at overwintering sites removes refuge trees.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Milkweed-containing grasslands, meadows, and fields across North America for breeding; overwintering in oyamel fir and pine-oak forest in the Trans-Mexican Volcanic Belt at 2,400–3,600 m elevation in Michoacán and Mexico State.
en.actions: Plant milkweed in gardens and on farms along the migration corridor, support World Wildlife Fund-Mexico's Sierra Chincua reserve protection, and advocate for milkweed-positive agricultural policies across the breeding range.

es.description: La mariposa monarca realiza una de las migraciones más extraordinarias de la naturaleza, viajando hasta 4.800 km desde Canadá y EE.UU. a las colonias de invernada en los bosques de abeto oyamel del centro de México. La población migratoria fue añadida a la Lista Roja de la UICN como En Peligro Crítico en 2022.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de asclepia por el uso de herbicidas y la agricultura en América del Norte ha eliminado la única planta que las orugas monarca pueden comer; los extremos climáticos interrumpen la migración; la tala ilegal en los sitios de invernada elimina los árboles refugio.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizales, prados y campos que contienen asclepia en América del Norte para la reproducción; invernada en bosques de abeto oyamel y pino-encino en el Cinturón Volcánico Trans-Mexicano a 2.400-3.600 m de altitud en Michoacán y el Estado de México.
es.actions: Plantar asclepia en jardines y granjas a lo largo del corredor de migración, apoyar la protección de la reserva Sierra Chincua de WWF-México y abogar por políticas agrícolas favorables a la asclepia en toda el área de reproducción.

pt.description: A borboleta-monarca realiza uma das mais extraordinárias migrações da natureza, viajando até 4.800 km do Canadá e dos EUA para as colônias de invernada nas florestas de abeto oyamel do centro do México. A população migratória foi adicionada à Lista Vermelha da IUCN como Criticamente Ameaçada em 2022.
pt.endangerment: A perda de asclépia pelo uso de herbicidas e pela agricultura na América do Norte eliminou a única planta que as lagartas monarca podem comer; os extremos climáticos interrompem a migração; o desmatamento ilegal nos locais de invernada remove as árvores de refúgio.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagens, prados e campos contendo asclépia na América do Norte para reprodução; invernada em florestas de abeto oyamel e pinheiro-carvalho no Cinturão Vulcânico Trans-Mexicano a 2.400-3.600 m de altitude em Michoacán e no Estado do México.
pt.actions: Plantar asclépia em jardins e fazendas ao longo do corredor de migração, apoiar a proteção da reserva Sierra Chincua da WWF-México e defender políticas agrícolas favoráveis à asclépia em toda a área de reprodução.

fr.description: Le papillon monarque effectue l'une des migrations les plus extraordinaires de la nature, parcourant jusqu'à 4 800 km du Canada et des États-Unis jusqu'aux colonies d'hivernage dans les forêts de sapins oyamel du centre du Mexique. La population migratrice a été ajoutée à la Liste rouge de l'UICN en tant qu'espèce en danger critique de disparition en 2022.
fr.endangerment: La perte d'asclépiade due à l'utilisation d'herbicides et à l'agriculture en Amérique du Nord a éliminé la seule plante que les chenilles monarques peuvent manger ; les extrêmes climatiques perturbent la migration ; l'exploitation forestière illégale sur les sites d'hivernage élimine les arbres refuges.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Prairies, prés et champs contenant de l'asclépiade en Amérique du Nord pour la reproduction ; hivernage dans les forêts de sapins oyamel et de pins-chênes dans la ceinture volcanique trans-mexicaine à 2 400-3 600 m d'altitude au Michoacán et dans l'État de Mexico.
fr.actions: Planter de l'asclépiade dans les jardins et les fermes le long du couloir de migration, soutenir la protection de la réserve de la Sierra Chincua du WWF-Mexique et plaider pour des politiques agricoles favorables à l'asclépiade dans toute l'aire de reproduction.

hi.endangerment: Loss of milkweed through herbicide use and कृषि in उत्तरी अमेरिका has removed the only योजनाt monarch caterpillars can eat; climate extremes disrupt migration; illegal लकड़ी काटना at overwintering sites removes refuge trees.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Milkweed-containing grasslands, meadows, and fields across उत्तरी अमेरिका for breeding; overwintering in oyamel fir and pine-oak वन in the Trans-Mexican Volcanic Belt at 2,400–3,600 m elevation in Michoacán and Mexico State.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The Indiana cave crayfish is a pale, eyeless freshwater crustacean adapted to the perpetual darkness of limestone cave streams in south-central Indiana. It represents an ancient lineage isolated underground since the Pleistocene.
en.endangerment: Agricultural runoff carrying pesticides, sediment, and fertilisers seeps into the karst groundwater system, poisoning cave streams; groundwater extraction for crop irrigation lowers water tables, drying cave pools.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Underground streams and cave pools in the karst limestone aquifer of south-central Indiana, with clean, cold, well-oxygenated groundwater, organic debris washed in from surface as food, and stable cave hydrology.
en.actions: Advocate for agricultural buffer zones along cave recharge areas in south-central Indiana, support Indiana Department of Natural Resources' cave ecosystem monitoring, and fund karst groundwater quality surveys.

es.description: El cangrejo de río de las cuevas de Indiana es un crustáceo de agua dulce pálido y sin ojos adaptado a la oscuridad perpetua de los arroyos subterráneos de piedra caliza en el centro-sur de Indiana. Representa un antiguo linaje aislado bajo tierra desde el Pleistoceno.
es.endangerment: La escorrentía agrícola que transporta pesticidas, sedimentos y fertilizantes se filtra en el sistema de aguas subterráneas kársticas, envenenando los arroyos de las cuevas; la extracción de aguas subterráneas para el riego de cultivos baja los niveles freáticos, secando las charcas subterráneas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos subterráneos y charcas de cuevas en el acuífero de piedra caliza kárstica del centro-sur de Indiana, con agua subterránea limpia, fría y bien oxigenada, detritos orgánicos arrastrados desde la superficie como alimento e hidrología estable de la cueva.
es.actions: Abogar por zonas de amortiguación agrícola a lo largo de las áreas de recarga de cuevas en el centro-sur de Indiana, apoyar el monitoreo del ecosistema de cuevas del Departamento de Recursos Naturales de Indiana y financiar encuestas de calidad de aguas subterráneas kársticas.

pt.description: O lagostim-de-caverna-de-indiana é um crustáceo de água doce pálido e sem olhos adaptado à escuridão perpétua dos riachos subterrâneos de calcário no centro-sul de Indiana. Representa uma linhagem antiga isolada no subsolo desde o Pleistoceno.
pt.endangerment: O escoamento agrícola que carrega pesticidas, sedimentos e fertilizantes penetra no sistema de águas subterrâneas cársticas, envenenando os riachos das cavernas; a extração de águas subterrâneas para irrigação de culturas abaixa os lençóis freáticos, secando as poças subterrâneas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Riachos subterrâneos e poças de cavernas no aquífero de calcário cárstico do centro-sul de Indiana, com água subterrânea limpa, fria e bem oxigenada, detritos orgânicos lavados da superfície como alimento e hidrologia estável da caverna.
pt.actions: Defender zonas de amortecimento agrícola ao longo das áreas de recarga de cavernas no centro-sul de Indiana, apoiar o monitoramento do ecossistema de cavernas do Departamento de Recursos Naturais de Indiana e financiar levantamentos de qualidade de águas subterrâneas cársticas.

fr.description: L'écrevisse des cavernes de l'Indiana est un crustacé d'eau douce pâle et sans yeux adapté à l'obscurité permanente des ruisseaux souterrains calcaires du centre-sud de l'Indiana. Elle représente une ancienne lignée isolée sous terre depuis le Pléistocène.
fr.endangerment: Le ruissellement agricole transportant des pesticides, des sédiments et des engrais s'infiltre dans le système d'eaux souterraines karstiques, empoisonnant les ruisseaux des grottes ; l'extraction des eaux souterraines pour l'irrigation agricole abaisse les nappes phréatiques, asséchant les mares souterraines.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux souterrains et mares de grottes dans l'aquifère calcaire karstique du centre-sud de l'Indiana, avec des eaux souterraines propres, froides et bien oxygénées, des débris organiques apportés de la surface comme nourriture et une hydrologie stable de la grotte.
fr.actions: Plaider pour des zones tampons agricoles le long des zones de recharge des grottes dans le centre-sud de l'Indiana, soutenir le suivi de l'écosystème des grottes du Département des ressources naturelles de l'Indiana et financer des enquêtes sur la qualité des eaux souterraines karstiques.

en.description: The baiji, or Yangtze River dolphin, was a freshwater dolphin endemic to the Yangtze River in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any individuals, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in modern times.
en.endangerment: Intensive river traffic, electrofishing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial pollution destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 individuals; by 2006, none could be found.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze River in central and eastern China with clear water, abundant fish, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky river conditions.
en.actions: Support the Yangtze River Protection Law's ban on fishing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving individuals, and back efforts to restore Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for river conservation.

es.description: El baiji, o delfín del Río Yangtze, era un delfín de río endémico del Río Yangtze en China. Una encuesta de 2006 no logró detectar ningún individuo, y ahora se considera funcionalmente extinto — el primer cetáceo conducido a la extinción en tiempos modernos.
es.endangerment: El intenso tráfico fluvial, la pesca eléctrica, la construcción de presas que bloqueaban la migración y la contaminación industrial destruyeron el ecosistema del Yangtze del que dependía el baiji. Una estimación de 1997 contó solo 13 individuos; para 2006, no se podía encontrar ninguno.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El canal principal del Río Yangtze en el centro y este de China con agua clara, abundantes peces, poco tráfico de barcos y tramos sin perturbaciones para la navegación basada en ecolocalización en condiciones de río turbio.
es.actions: Apoyar la prohibición de pesca de la Ley de Protección del Río Yangtze hasta 2030, financiar encuestas de ADN ambiental para cualquier individuo superviviente y respaldar los esfuerzos para restaurar la salud del ecosistema del Yangtze como memorial y modelo para la conservación fluvial.

pt.description: O baiji, ou golfinho-do-rio-yangtze, era um golfinho fluvial endêmico do Rio Yangtze na China. Uma pesquisa de 2006 não detectou nenhum indivíduo, e agora é considerado funcionalmente extinto — o primeiro cetáceo conduzido à extinção nos tempos modernos.
pt.endangerment: O intenso tráfego fluvial, a pesca elétrica, a construção de barragens bloqueando a migração e a poluição industrial destruíram o ecossistema do Yangtze do qual o baiji dependia. Uma estimativa de 1997 contou apenas 13 indivíduos; em 2006, nenhum pôde ser encontrado.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O canal principal do Rio Yangtze no centro e leste da China com água clara, peixes abundantes, baixo tráfego de barcos e trechos sem perturbações para a navegação baseada em ecolocalização em condições de rio turvo.
pt.actions: Apoiar a proibição de pesca da Lei de Proteção do Rio Yangtze até 2030, financiar levantamentos de DNA ambiental para qualquer indivíduo sobrevivente e apoiar os esforços para restaurar a saúde do ecossistema do Yangtze como memorial e modelo para a conservação fluvial.

fr.description: Le baiji, ou dauphin du fleuve Yangtsé, était un dauphin fluvial endémique du fleuve Yangtsé en Chine. Une enquête menée en 2006 n'a détecté aucun individu, et il est maintenant considéré comme fonctionnellement éteint — le premier cétacé conduit à l'extinction dans les temps modernes.
fr.endangerment: L'intense trafic fluvial, la pêche électrique, la construction de barrages bloquant la migration et la pollution industrielle ont détruit l'écosystème du Yangtsé dont dépendait le baiji. Une estimation de 1997 en comptait seulement 13 ; en 2006, aucun n'a pu être trouvé.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Le chenal principal du fleuve Yangtsé dans le centre et l'est de la Chine avec de l'eau claire, des poissons abondants, un faible trafic de bateaux et des tronçons non perturbés pour la navigation par écholocation dans les eaux turbides du fleuve.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'interdiction de pêche de la loi sur la protection du fleuve Yangtsé jusqu'en 2030, financer des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental pour tout individu survivant et soutenir les efforts de restauration de la santé de l'écosystème du Yangtsé comme mémorial et modèle pour la conservation des rivières.

ja.description: The baiji, or Yangtze 河川 dolphin, was a 淡水 dolphin 固有 to the Yangtze 河川 in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any 個体, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to 絶滅 in 現代 times.
ja.endangerment: Intensive 川 traffic, electro魚ing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial 汚染 破壊 the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 個体; by 2006, none could be 見つけた.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze 河川 in central and eastern China with 明確 water, 豊富 魚, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky 川 conditions.
ja.actions: Support the Yangtze 河川 Protection Law's ban on 魚ing until 2030, fund 環境 DNA surveys for any surviving 個体, and back 努力s to 回復 Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for 川 保全.

zh.description: The baiji, or Yangtze 河流 dolphin, was a 淡水 dolphin 特有 to the Yangtze 河流 in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any 个体, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in 现代 times.
zh.endangerment: Intensive 河流 traffic, electro鱼类ing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial 污染 destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 个体; by 2006, none could be found.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze 河流 in central and eastern China with clear water, 丰富 鱼类, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky 河流 conditions.
zh.actions: Support the Yangtze 河流 Protection Law's ban on 鱼类ing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving 个体, and back 努力s to 恢复 Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for 河流 保护.

ar.description: The baiji, or Yangtze نهر dolphin, was a مياه عذبة dolphin متصف ذاتياً to the Yangtze نهر in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any أفراد, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in حديث times.
ar.endangerment: Intensive نهر traffic, electroأسماكing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial التلوث destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 أفراد; by 2006, none could be found.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze نهر in central and eastern China with clear water, وفير أسماك, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky نهر conditions.
ar.actions: Support the Yangtze نهر Protection Law's ban on أسماكing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving أفراد, and back جهدs to يستعيد Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for نهر الحفظ.

hi.description: The baiji, or Yangtze नदी dolphin, was a मीठा पानी dolphin स्थानिक to the Yangtze नदी in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any व्यक्ति, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in आधुनिक times.
hi.endangerment: Intensive नदी traffic, electroमछलीing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial प्रदूषण destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 व्यक्ति; by 2006, none could be found.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze नदी in central and eastern China with clear water, प्रचुर मछली, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky नदी conditions.
hi.actions: Support the Yangtze नदी Protection Law's ban on मछलीing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving व्यक्ति, and back प्रयासs to पुनर्स्थापना Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for नदी संरक्षण.

en.description: The North Atlantic right whale is among the world's most endangered large whales, with fewer than 370 individuals remaining. Once targeted by whalers for its high blubber content and tendency to float when killed, giving it its name as the "right" whale to hunt.
en.endangerment: Rope entanglement in fishing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher fishing activity.
en.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic coastal and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in cold, productive shelf waters and using protected bays for calving.
en.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, advocate for ropeless fishing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

es.description: La ballena franca del Atlántico Norte está entre las ballenas grandes más amenazadas del mundo, con menos de 370 individuos restantes. Antes cazada por los balleneros por su alto contenido de grasa y su tendencia a flotar cuando era matada, dándole su nombre como la ballena 'correcta' para cazar.
es.endangerment: El enredo en cuerdas de equipos de pesca —particularmente las líneas de trampas de langosta— y las colisiones con barcos matan varias ballenas cada año, una tasa que supera la reproducción. El cambio climático está desplazando las presas hacia áreas con mayor actividad pesquera.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras y de alta mar del Atlántico Norte desde Florida hasta el Golfo de San Lorenzo, alimentándose de densas agregaciones de copépodos en aguas frías y productivas de la plataforma continental y usando bahías protegidas para el parto.
es.actions: Apoyar la respuesta de enredo de ballenas grandes de la NOAA, abogar por la adopción de equipos de pesca sin cuerdas en el noreste de EE.UU. y respaldar restricciones de velocidad obligatorias para los barcos en el hábitat de la ballena franca.

pt.description: A baleia-franca-do-atlântico-norte está entre as maiores baleias mais ameaçadas do mundo, com menos de 370 indivíduos restantes. Antes alvo dos baleeiros por seu alto teor de gordura e tendência a flutuar quando morta, dando-lhe o nome de baleia 'certa' para caçar.
pt.endangerment: O emaranhamento em cordas de equipamentos de pesca — particularmente linhas de armadilhas de lagosta — e colisões com navios matam várias baleias a cada ano, uma taxa que supera a reprodução. As mudanças climáticas estão deslocando as presas para áreas com maior atividade pesqueira.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras e ao largo do Atlântico Norte da Flórida ao Golfo de São Lourenço, alimentando-se de densas agregações de copépodos em águas frias e produtivas da plataforma continental e usando baías protegidas para o parto.
pt.actions: Apoiar a resposta de emaranhamento de grandes baleias da NOAA, defender a adoção de equipamentos de pesca sem cordas no nordeste dos EUA e apoiar restrições de velocidade obrigatórias para navios no habitat da baleia-franca.

fr.description: La baleine noire de l'Atlantique Nord figure parmi les grandes baleines les plus menacées du monde, avec moins de 370 individus restants. Autrefois ciblée par les baleiniers pour sa forte teneur en graisse et sa tendance à flotter lorsqu'elle était tuée, lui donnant son nom de baleine « juste » à chasser.
fr.endangerment: L'enchevêtrement dans les cordages des engins de pêche — notamment les lignes des casiers à homards — et les collisions de navires tuent plusieurs baleines chaque année, à un rythme qui dépasse la reproduction. Le changement climatique déplace les proies vers des zones à plus forte activité de pêche.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux côtières et hauturières de l'Atlantique Nord de la Floride au golfe du Saint-Laurent, se nourrissant de denses agrégations de copépodes dans les eaux froides et productives du plateau continental et utilisant des baies abritées pour les mises bas.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de réponse aux enchevêtrements de grandes baleines de la NOAA, plaider pour l'adoption d'engins de pêche sans cordages dans le nord-est des États-Unis et soutenir des restrictions de vitesse obligatoires pour les navires dans l'habitat de la baleine noire.

ja.endangerment: Rope entanglement in 魚ing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every 年, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher 魚ing activity.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic 海岸al and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in 寒い, productive shelf waters and using 保護ed bays for calving.
ja.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, 提唱 for ropeless 魚ing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

zh.description: The North Atlantic right whale is among the 世界's most 濒危 large whales, with 少于 370 个体 remaining. Once targeted by whalers for its high blubber content and tendency to float when killed, giving it its name as the "right" whale to hunt.
zh.endangerment: Rope entanglement in 鱼类ing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher 鱼类ing activity.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic 海岸al and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in 寒冷, productive shelf waters and using 保护ed bays for calving.
zh.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, 倡导 for ropeless 鱼类ing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

ar.endangerment: Rope entanglement in أسماكing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher أسماكing activity.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic ساحلal and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in بارد, productive shelf waters and using يحميed bays for calving.
ar.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, يدافع for ropeless أسماكing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

hi.description: The North Atlantic right whale is among the विश्व's most लुप्तप्राय large whales, with कम से कम 370 व्यक्ति remaining. Once targeted by whalers for its high blubber content and tendency to float when killed, giving it its name as the "right" whale to hunt.
hi.endangerment: Rope entanglement in मछलीing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher मछलीing activity.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic तटal and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in ठंडा, productive shelf waters and using सुरक्षाed bays for calving.
hi.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, समर्थन for ropeless मछलीing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

en.description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea forests of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining populations in Côte d'Ivoire.
en.endangerment: Bushmeat hunting, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in local markets, has devastated populations across both countries; simultaneous deforestation for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane tropical rainforest in the Upper Guinea biodiversity hotspot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and protection from hunting disturbance.
en.actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-poaching efforts in the Tanoé-Ehy forest in Côte d'Ivoire, advocate for community-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

es.description: El mono roloway es un primate llamativo con una distintiva barba blanca y cara oscura, encontrado en los bosques de la Alta Guinea de Côte d'Ivoire y Ghana. Los estudios sugieren que puede estar funcionalmente extinto en Ghana con solo unas pocas poblaciones restantes en Côte d'Ivoire.
es.endangerment: La caza de carne de monte, considerando los monos roloway un manjar en los mercados locales, ha devastado las poblaciones en ambos países; la deforestación simultánea para el cultivo de cacao ha eliminado los corredores de hábitat restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Selva tropical de tierras bajas y montana intacta en el hotspot de biodiversidad de la Alta Guinea en el sur de Côte d'Ivoire, con acceso a árboles frutales, un dosel cerrado y protección contra la perturbación de la caza.
es.actions: Apoyar el centro de rescate del mono roloway de WAPCA y los esfuerzos anti-furtivismo en el bosque Tanoé-Ehy de Côte d'Ivoire, abogar por la reducción comunitaria de la carne de monte y financiar encuestas sistemáticas del área.

pt.description: O macaco-roloway é um primata impressionante com uma barba branca distintiva e rosto escuro, encontrado nas florestas da Alta Guiné de Côte d'Ivoire e Gana. Os levantamentos sugerem que pode estar funcionalmente extinto em Gana, com apenas algumas populações restantes em Côte d'Ivoire.
pt.endangerment: A caça de carne de caça, considerando os macacos roloway uma iguaria nos mercados locais, devastou as populações em ambos os países; o desmatamento simultâneo para o cultivo de cacau eliminou os corredores de habitat restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de baixadas e montana intacta no hotspot de biodiversidade da Alta Guiné no sul de Côte d'Ivoire, com acesso a árvores frutíferas, um dossel fechado e proteção contra perturbações de caça.
pt.actions: Apoiar o centro de resgate do macaco roloway da WAPCA e os esforços antipirataria na floresta Tanoé-Ehy de Côte d'Ivoire, defender a redução comunitária da carne de caça e financiar levantamentos sistemáticos da área.

fr.description: Le colobe de Roloway est un primate remarquable à la barbe blanche distinctive et au visage sombre, trouvé dans les forêts de Haute Guinée de Côte d'Ivoire et du Ghana. Les études suggèrent qu'il pourrait être fonctionnellement éteint au Ghana, avec seulement quelques populations restantes en Côte d'Ivoire.
fr.endangerment: La chasse à la viande de brousse, les singes roloway étant considérés comme un mets délicat sur les marchés locaux, a dévasté les populations dans les deux pays ; la déforestation simultanée pour la culture du cacao a éliminé les corridors d'habitat restants.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide de plaine et de montagne intacte dans le hotspot de biodiversité de Haute Guinée dans le sud de la Côte d'Ivoire, avec accès à des arbres fruitiers, une canopée fermée et une protection contre les perturbations liées à la chasse.
fr.actions: Soutenir le centre de sauvetage du colobe de Roloway de WAPCA et les efforts anti-braconnage dans la forêt de Tanoé-Ehy en Côte d'Ivoire, plaider pour la réduction de la viande de brousse au niveau communautaire et financer des enquêtes systématiques sur l'aire de répartition.

zh.description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea 森林s of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining 种群s in Côte d'Ivoire.
zh.endangerment: Bushmeat 狩猎, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in 地方 markets, has devastated 种群s across both countries; simultaneous 森林砍伐 for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane 热带 rain森林 in the Upper Guinea biodiversity 炎热spot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and 保护ion from 狩猎 disturbance.
zh.actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-偷猎 努力s in the Tanoé-Ehy 森林 in Côte d'Ivoire, 倡导 for 社区-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

ar.description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea غابةs of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining عدد السكانs in Côte d'Ivoire.
ar.endangerment: Bushmeat الصيد, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in محلي markets, has devastated عدد السكانs across both countries; simultaneous إزالة الغابات for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane استوائي rainغابة in the Upper Guinea biodiversity حارspot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and يحميion from الصيد disturbance.
ar.actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-الصيد الجائر جهدs in the Tanoé-Ehy غابة in Côte d'Ivoire, يدافع for مجتمع-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

hi.description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea वनs of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining जनसंख्याs in Côte d'Ivoire.
hi.endangerment: Bushmeat शिकार, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in स्थानीय markets, has devastated जनसंख्याs across both countries; simultaneous वनों की कटाई for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane उष्णकटिबंधीय rainवन in the Upper Guinea biodiversity गर्मीspot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and सुरक्षाion from शिकार disturbance.
hi.actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-अवैध शिकार प्रयासs in the Tanoé-Ehy वन in Côte d'Ivoire, समर्थन for समुदाय-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The pygmy hog is the world's smallest wild pig, barely 25 cm tall and weighing around 8 kg. Once distributed across the Terai grasslands from Nepal through Bhutan to Assam, it now survives only in Manas National Park and a handful of adjacent reserves in Assam, India.
en.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to agriculture and human settlement eliminated the species from all but a fraction of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense stands of tall elephant grass (Saccharum spontaneum and Narenga porphyrocoma) in the terai floodplain landscape of Assam, with undisturbed nesting habitat and seasonal wetland areas.
en.actions: Support the Pygmy Hog Conservation Programme's captive breeding and reintroduction work in Assam, advocate for controlled burning regimes that preserve tall grass patches in Manas and Orang, and fund habitat corridor restoration.

es.description: El cerdo pigmeo es el cerdo salvaje más pequeño del mundo, con apenas 25 cm de altura y un peso de unos 8 kg. Antes distribuido en los pastizales de Terai desde Nepal a través de Bután hasta Assam, ahora sobrevive solo en el Parque Nacional Manas y un puñado de reservas adyacentes en Assam, India.
es.endangerment: La conversión del hábitat de hierba elefante alta y pantano terai en agricultura y asentamientos humanos eliminó la especie de todo menos una fracción de su área; las quemas anuales destruyen los parches de pastizal restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Densos rodales de hierba elefante alta (Saccharum spontaneum y Narenga porphyrocoma) en el paisaje de llanura aluvial terai de Assam, con hábitat de anidamiento sin perturbaciones y áreas de humedales estacionales.
es.actions: Apoyar el trabajo de cría en cautiverio y reintroducción del Programa de Conservación del Cerdo Pigmeo en Assam, abogar por regímenes de quema controlada que preserven parches de hierba alta en Manas y Orang y financiar la restauración de corredores de hábitat.

pt.description: O porco-pigmeu é o menor porco selvagem do mundo, com apenas 25 cm de altura e pesando cerca de 8 kg. Antes distribuído nas pastagens de Terai do Nepal ao Butão e Assam, agora sobrevive apenas no Parque Nacional Manas e em algumas reservas adjacentes em Assam, Índia.
pt.endangerment: A conversão do habitat de capim-elefante alto e pântano terai em agricultura e assentamentos humanos eliminou a espécie de tudo menos uma fração de sua área; as queimadas anuais destroem os fragmentos de pastagem restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Densos agrupamentos de capim-elefante alto (Saccharum spontaneum e Narenga porphyrocoma) na paisagem de planície aluvial terai de Assam, com habitat de nidificação sem perturbações e áreas de zonas úmidas sazonais.
pt.actions: Apoiar o trabalho de criação em cativeiro e reintrodução do Programa de Conservação do Porco-Pigmeu em Assam, defender regimes de queima controlada que preservem fragmentos de capim alto em Manas e Orang e financiar a restauração de corredores de habitat.

fr.description: Le cochon pygmée est le plus petit cochon sauvage du monde, à peine 25 cm de haut et pesant environ 8 kg. Autrefois réparti dans les prairies du Teraï du Népal au Bhoutan et jusqu'en Assam, il ne survit aujourd'hui que dans le parc national de Manas et une poignée de réserves adjacentes en Assam, en Inde.
fr.endangerment: La conversion de l'habitat de hautes herbes éléphantes et de marais du Teraï en terres agricoles et en colonies humaines a éliminé l'espèce de presque toute son aire ; les brûlis annuels détruisent les parcelles de prairie restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Denses peuplements de hautes herbes éléphantes (Saccharum spontaneum et Narenga porphyrocoma) dans le paysage de plaine alluviale du Teraï en Assam, avec un habitat de nidification non perturbé et des zones humides saisonnières.
fr.actions: Soutenir les travaux d'élevage en captivité et de réintroduction du Programme de conservation du cochon pygmée en Assam, plaider pour des régimes de brûlage contrôlé préservant les parcelles de hautes herbes à Manas et Orang et financer la restauration des corridors d'habitat.

ja.description: The pygmy hog is the 世界's 最小 野生 pig, barely 25 cm tall and weighing around 8 kg. Once distributed across the Terai grasslands from Nepal through Bhutan to Assam, it now survives only in Manas National Park and a handful of adjacent reserves in Assam, India.
ja.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to 農業 and human settlement eliminated the 種 from all but a fr行動 of its range; 一年生 burning destroys remaining grassland patches.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dense stands of tall elephant grass (Saccharum spontaneum and Narenga porphyrocoma) in the terai floodplain landscape of Assam, with undisturbed nesting habitat and 季節 湿ったland areas.
ja.actions: Support the Pygmy Hog Conservation Programme's 人死亡 and 再導入 work in Assam, 提唱 for controlled burning regimes that preserve tall grass patches in Manas and Orang, and fund habitat corridor 修復.

zh.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to 农业 and human settlement eliminated the 物种 from all but a fr行动 of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Dense stands of tall elephant grass (Saccharum spontaneum and Narenga porphyrocoma) in the terai floodplain landscape of Assam, with undisturbed nesting habitat and 季节性 潮湿land areas.
zh.actions: Support the Pygmy Hog Conservation Programme's 圈养繁殖 and 重新引入 work in Assam, 倡导 for controlled burning regimes that preserve tall grass patches in Manas and Orang, and fund habitat corridor 恢复.

ar.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to الزراعة and human settlement eliminated the الأنواع from all but a frإجراء of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.

hi.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to कृषि and human settlement eliminated the प्रजातियाँ from all but a frकार्रवाई of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.

en.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of South America's rarest birds. Estimated at fewer than 250 mature individuals, it occupies fast-flowing, clear rivers in the Brazilian Cerrado.
en.endangerment: Siltation from agriculture, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery forest have devastated the clear, fast-flowing rivers this specialist species depends on for feeding and nesting.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine rivers with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery forest in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic Forest, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.
en.actions: Support ICMBio's Brazilian merganser action plan in Serra da Canastra and Chapada dos Veadeiros, advocate for strict riparian forest protection along Cerrado rivers, and fund dam impact assessments.

es.description: El mergo brasileño es un gran pato zambullidor de pico serrado y una de las aves más raras de América del Sur. Estimado en menos de 250 individuos maduros, ocupa ríos de rápido flujo y claros en el Cerrado brasileño.
es.endangerment: La sedimentación de la agricultura, la construcción de presas hidroeléctricas y la destrucción del bosque galería ripario han devastado los ríos claros y de rápido flujo de los que depende esta especie especialista para alimentarse y anidar.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos rápidos, claros y prístinos con canales rocosos y bosque galería bien conservado en el Cerrado brasileño y el adyacente Bosque Atlántico, particularmente los afluentes del São Francisco y Tocantins en Goiás.
es.actions: Apoyar el plan de acción del mergo brasileño del ICMBio en Serra da Canastra y Chapada dos Veadeiros, abogar por una estricta protección del bosque ripario a lo largo de los ríos del Cerrado y financiar evaluaciones de impacto de presas.

pt.description: O mergão-brasileiro é um grande pato mergulhador de bico serrilhado e uma das aves mais raras da América do Sul. Estimado em menos de 250 indivíduos maduros, ocupa rios de fluxo rápido e claros no Cerrado brasileiro.
pt.endangerment: O assoreamento pela agricultura, a construção de barragens hidrelétricas e a destruição da floresta galeria ripária devastaram os rios claros e de fluxo rápido dos quais esta espécie especialista depende para se alimentar e nidificar.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios rápidos, claros e pristinos com canais rochosos e floresta galeria bem conservada no Cerrado brasileiro e na Mata Atlântica adjacente, particularmente os afluentes do São Francisco e Tocantins em Goiás.
pt.actions: Apoiar o plano de ação do mergão-brasileiro do ICMBio na Serra da Canastra e na Chapada dos Veadeiros, defender a proteção rigorosa da floresta ripária ao longo dos rios do Cerrado e financiar avaliações de impacto de barragens.

fr.description: Le harle du Brésil est un grand canard plongeur à bec dentelé et l'un des oiseaux les plus rares d'Amérique du Sud. Estimé à moins de 250 individus matures, il occupe des rivières au courant rapide et clair dans le Cerrado brésilien.
fr.endangerment: L'envasement dû à l'agriculture, la construction de barrages hydroélectriques et la destruction de la forêt galerie riveraine ont dévasté les rivières claires et à courant rapide dont dépend cette espèce spécialisée pour se nourrir et nicher.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières rapides, claires et vierges avec des chenaux rocheux et une forêt galerie bien préservée dans le Cerrado brésilien et la Forêt Atlantique adjacente, notamment les affluents du São Francisco et du Tocantins dans le Goiás.
fr.actions: Soutenir le plan d'action du harle du Brésil de l'ICMBio à Serra da Canastra et Chapada dos Veadeiros, plaider pour une protection stricte des forêts riveraines le long des rivières du Cerrado et financer des évaluations d'impact des barrages.

ja.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of 南アメリカ's まれst 鳥. Estimated at 未満 250 mature 個体, it occupies fast-flowing, 明確 川s in the Brazilian Cerrado.
ja.endangerment: Siltation from 農業, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery 森林 have devastated the 明確, fast-flowing 川s this specialist 種 depends on for feeding and nesting.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, 明確, pristine 川s with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery 森林 in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic 森林, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.
ja.actions: Support ICMBio's Brazilian merganser 行動 計画 in Serra da Canastra and Chapada dos Veadeiros, 提唱 for strict riparian 森林 保護ion along Cerrado 川s, and fund dam impact 評価ments.

zh.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of 南美洲's 稀有st 鸟类. Estimated at 少于 250 mature 个体, it occupies fast-flowing, clear 河流s in the Brazilian Cerrado.
zh.endangerment: Siltation from 农业, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery 森林 have devastated the clear, fast-flowing 河流s this specialist 物种 depends on for feeding and nesting.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine 河流s with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery 森林 in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic 森林, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.
zh.actions: Support ICMBio's Brazilian merganser 行动 计划 in Serra da Canastra and Chapada dos Veadeiros, 倡导 for strict riparian 森林 保护ion along Cerrado 河流s, and fund dam impact 评估ments.

ar.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of أمريكا الجنوبية's نادرst طيور. Estimated at أقل من 250 mature أفراد, it occupies fast-flowing, clear نهرs in the Brazilian Cerrado.
ar.endangerment: Siltation from الزراعة, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery غابة have devastated the clear, fast-flowing نهرs this specialist الأنواع depends on for feeding and nesting.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine نهرs with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery غابة in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic غابة, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.

hi.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of दक्षिण अमेरिका's दुर्लभst पक्षी. Estimated at कम से कम 250 mature व्यक्ति, it occupies fast-flowing, clear नदीs in the Brazilian Cerrado.

hi.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine नदीs with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery वन in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic वन, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.

en.description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite small dove with iridescent blue eyes, endemic to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 years, it was spectacularly rediscovered in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
en.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost more than half its natural vegetation to agriculture; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in South America.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open campo limpo grassland with sparse native vegetation in the Brazilian Cerrado, particularly disturbed areas with mixed grass and bare ground where it forages for small seeds at ground level.
en.actions: Support BirdLife Brazil's campo limpo protection programme in Minas Gerais, advocate for RPPN (private reserve) establishment in the rediscovery area, and fund population monitoring and habitat mapping.

es.description: La palomita de ojos azules es una exquisita paloma pequeña con ojos azules iridiscentes, endémica del Cerrado brasileño. Considerada extinta durante 75 años, fue espectacularmente redescubierta en 2015 en un diminuto fragmento de Cerrado nativo en Minas Gerais.
es.endangerment: El Cerrado brasileño ha perdido más de la mitad de su vegetación natural en la agricultura; el pastizal nativo de campo limpo —el hábitat específico que esta paloma necesita— es uno de los tipos de vegetación más amenazados en América del Sur.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizal abierto de campo limpo con vegetación nativa escasa en el Cerrado brasileño, particularmente áreas perturbadas con mezcla de hierba y suelo descubierto donde forrajea semillas pequeñas a nivel del suelo.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de protección de campo limpo de BirdLife Brasil en Minas Gerais, abogar por el establecimiento de RPPN (reserva privada) en el área de redescubrimiento y financiar el monitoreo de población y la cartografía de hábitats.

pt.description: A rolinha-dos-olhos-azuis é uma exquisita pombinha com olhos azuis iridescentes, endêmica do Cerrado brasileiro. Considerada extinta por 75 anos, foi espetacularmente redescoberta em 2015 em um minúsculo fragmento de Cerrado nativo em Minas Gerais.
pt.endangerment: O Cerrado brasileiro perdeu mais da metade de sua vegetação natural para a agricultura; a pastagem nativa de campo limpo — o habitat específico que esta pombinha necessita — é um dos tipos de vegetação mais ameaçados da América do Sul.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagem aberta de campo limpo com vegetação nativa esparsa no Cerrado brasileiro, particularmente áreas perturbadas com mistura de grama e solo descoberto onde forrageia pequenas sementes ao nível do solo.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de proteção de campo limpo da BirdLife Brasil em Minas Gerais, defender o estabelecimento de RPPN (reserva particular) na área de redescoberta e financiar o monitoramento populacional e o mapeamento de habitats.

fr.description: La colombe à yeux bleus est une exquise petite colombe aux yeux bleus iridescents, endémique du Cerrado brésilien. Considérée comme éteinte pendant 75 ans, elle a été spectaculairement redécouverte en 2015 dans un minuscule fragment de Cerrado naturel dans le Minas Gerais.
fr.endangerment: Le Cerrado brésilien a perdu plus de la moitié de sa végétation naturelle au profit de l'agriculture ; les prairies indigènes de campo limpo — l'habitat spécifique dont a besoin cette colombe — figurent parmi les types de végétation les plus menacés d'Amérique du Sud.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Prairie ouverte de campo limpo avec une végétation indigène clairsemée dans le Cerrado brésilien, notamment des zones perturbées avec un mélange d'herbes et de sol nu où elle cherche de petites graines au niveau du sol.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de protection du campo limpo de BirdLife Brésil dans le Minas Gerais, plaider pour l'établissement de RPPN (réserve privée) dans la zone de redécouverte et financer le suivi des populations et la cartographie de l'habitat.

ja.description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite 小さい dove with iridescent blue eyes, 固有 to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 年s, it was spectacularly re発見ed in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
ja.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has 失われた 以上 half its natural vegetation to 農業; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in 南アメリカ.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Open campo limpo grassland with sparse native vegetation in the Brazilian Cerrado, particularly disturbed areas with mixed grass and bare ground where it forages for 小さい seeds at ground level.
ja.actions: Support BirdLife Brazil's campo limpo 保護ion プログラムme in Minas Gerais, 提唱 for RPPN (private reserve) establishment in the re発見y area, and fund 個体数 監視ing and habitat mapping.

zh.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost 多于 half its natural vegetation to 农业; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in 南美洲.

ar.description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite صغير dove with iridescent blue eyes, متصف ذاتياً to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 years, it was spectacularly reيكتشفed in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
ar.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost أكثر من half its natural vegetation to الزراعة; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in أمريكا الجنوبية.

hi.description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite छोटा dove with iridescent blue eyes, स्थानिक to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 years, it was spectacularly reखोजेंed in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
hi.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost अधिक से अधिक half its natural vegetation to कृषि; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in दक्षिण अमेरिका.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Open campo limpo grassland with sparse native vegetation in the Brazilian Cerrado, particularly disturbed areas with mixed grass and bare ground where it forages for छोटा seeds at ground level.
hi.actions: Support BirdLife Brazil's campo limpo सुरक्षाion कार्यक्रमme in Minas Gerais, समर्थन for RPPN (private reserve) establishment in the reखोजेंy area, and fund जनसंख्या निगरानीing and habitat mapping.
