en.description: The Marquesan kingfisher is found only on the island of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an estimated population of just 400–600 individuals, it represents one of the Pacific's most threatened forest birds.
en.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; habitat loss from agriculture and invasive vegetation transforms the native coastal and valley forest the species depends on.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dry coastal and valley forest on Tahuata island in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging invertebrates and small lizards.
en.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird programme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and advocate for strict protection of remaining native forest on the island.

es.description: El martín pescador de las Marquesas solo se encuentra en la isla de Tahuata en las Islas Marquesas, Polinesia Francesa. Con una población estimada de solo 400-600 individuos, representa una de las aves forestales más amenazadas del Pacífico.
es.endangerment: Los gatos y ratas introducidos depredan huevos y polluelos de las cavidades de anidamiento; la pérdida de hábitat por la agricultura y la vegetación invasora transforma el bosque costero y de valles nativo del que depende la especie.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque costero seco y de valles en la isla Tahuata en las Marquesas, particularmente áreas con árboles muertos de gran diámetro que contienen cavidades naturales para anidar y suelo abierto para forrajear invertebrados y pequeños lagartos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de aves de las Marquesas de SOP Manu, financiar el control de depredadores invasores en Tahuata usando trampas y estaciones de veneno y abogar por la estricta protección del bosque nativo restante en la isla.

pt.description: O martim-pescador-das-marquesas é encontrado apenas na ilha de Tahuata nas Ilhas Marquesas, Polinésia Francesa. Com uma população estimada de apenas 400-600 indivíduos, representa uma das aves florestais mais ameaçadas do Pacífico.
pt.endangerment: Gatos e ratos introduzidos predam ovos e filhotes das cavidades de nidificação; a perda de habitat pela agricultura e vegetação invasora transforma a floresta costeira e de vales nativa da qual a espécie depende.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta costeira seca e de vales na ilha de Tahuata nas Marquesas, particularmente áreas com árvores mortas de grande diâmetro contendo cavidades naturais para nidificação e terreno aberto para forragear invertebrados e pequenos lagartos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de aves das Marquesas da SOP Manu, financiar o controle de predadores invasores em Tahuata usando armadilhas e estações de veneno e defender a proteção rigorosa da floresta nativa restante na ilha.

fr.description: Le martin-pêcheur des Marquises ne se trouve que sur l'île de Tahuata dans les Îles Marquises, en Polynésie française. Avec une population estimée à seulement 400-600 individus, il représente l'un des oiseaux forestiers les plus menacés du Pacifique.
fr.endangerment: Les chats et rats introduits prédatent les œufs et les poussins dans les cavités de nidification ; la perte d'habitat due à l'agriculture et à la végétation envahissante transforme la forêt côtière et de vallée indigène dont dépend l'espèce.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt côtière sèche et de vallée sur l'île de Tahuata aux Marquises, notamment des zones avec de grands arbres morts contenant des cavités naturelles pour la nidification et des zones ouvertes pour chercher des invertébrés et de petits lézards.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme des oiseaux marquisiens de SOP Manu, financer le contrôle des prédateurs envahissants sur Tahuata en utilisant des pièges et des stations d'appâts empoisonnés et plaider pour une protection stricte de la forêt indigène restante sur l'île.

ja.description: The Marquesan king魚er is 見つけた only on the 島 of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an 推定個体数 of just 400–600 個体, it re現在s one of the Pacific's most threatened 森林 鳥.
ja.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; 生息地の丧失 from 農業 and invasive vegetation transforms the native 海岸al and valley 森林 the 種 depends on.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Dry 海岸al and valley 森林 on Tahuata 島 in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for 採取 無脊椎動物 and 小さい lizards.
ja.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird プログラムme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and 提唱 for strict 保護ion of remaining native 森林 on the 島.

zh.description: The Marquesan king鱼类er is found only on the 岛屿 of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an 估计种群 of just 400–600 个体, it re现在s one of the Pacific's most threatened 森林 鸟类.
zh.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; 栖息地丧失 from 农业 and invasive vegetation transforms the native 海岸al and valley 森林 the 物种 depends on.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Dry 海岸al and valley 森林 on Tahuata 岛屿 in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging 无脊椎动物 and 小型 lizards.
zh.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird 项目me, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and 倡导 for strict 保护ion of remaining native 森林 on the 岛屿.

ar.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; فقدان الموطن from الزراعة and invasive vegetation transforms the native ساحلal and valley غابة the الأنواع depends on.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Dry ساحلal and valley غابة on Tahuata جزيرة in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging لافقاريات and صغير lizards.
ar.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird برنامجme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and يدافع for strict يحميion of remaining native غابة on the جزيرة.

hi.description: The Marquesan kingमछलीer is found only on the द्वीप of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an अनुमानित जनसंख्या of just 400–600 व्यक्ति, it reवर्तमानs one of the Pacific's most threatened वन पक्षी.
hi.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; आवास की हानि from कृषि and invasive vegetation transforms the native तटal and valley वन the प्रजातियाँ depends on.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Dry तटal and valley वन on Tahuata द्वीप in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging अकशेरुकी and छोटा lizards.
hi.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird कार्यक्रमme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and समर्थन for strict सुरक्षाion of remaining native वन on the द्वीप.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the islands of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened birds in Southeast Asia.
en.endangerment: Deforestation for agriculture has eliminated more than 95% of lowland forest on Negros and Panay; remaining forest is heavily hunted, and the species is also captured for the cagebird trade.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill forest on Negros and Panay, particularly forest with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
en.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's forest protection and restoration in the North Negros Natural Park, advocate for hunting bans in remaining forest fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining populations.

es.description: La paloma corazón sangrante de Negros es una paloma terrestre llamada así por la vívida 'mancha de sangre' roja en su pecho blanco. Restringida a las islas de Negros y Panay en Filipinas, está entre las aves más amenazadas del sureste de Asia.
es.endangerment: La deforestación para la agricultura ha eliminado más del 95% del bosque de tierras bajas en Negros y Panay; el bosque restante es intensamente cazado, y la especie también es capturada para el comercio de aves en jaula.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque primario y secundario maduro intacto y sin perturbaciones de tierras bajas y estribaciones en Negros y Panay, particularmente bosque con denso sotobosque, hojarasca para forrajeo y mínima perturbación humana.
es.actions: Apoyar la protección y restauración forestal de la Fundación Haribon en el Parque Natural del Norte de Negros, abogar por prohibiciones de caza en los fragmentos forestales restantes y financiar encuestas para localizar las poblaciones restantes.

pt.description: A pomba-coração-sangrante-de-negros é uma pomba terrestre chamada assim pela vívida 'mancha de sangue' vermelha em seu peito branco. Restrita às ilhas de Negros e Panay nas Filipinas, está entre as aves mais ameaçadas do Sudeste Asiático.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para a agricultura eliminou mais de 95% da floresta de baixadas em Negros e Panay; a floresta restante é intensamente caçada, e a espécie também é capturada para o comércio de pássaros em gaiola.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta primária e secundária madura intacta e sem perturbações de baixadas e encostas em Negros e Panay, particularmente floresta com sub-bosque denso, serapilheira para forragear e perturbação humana mínima.
pt.actions: Apoiar a proteção e restauração florestal da Fundação Haribon no Parque Natural do Norte de Negros, defender proibições de caça nos fragmentos florestais restantes e financiar levantamentos para localizar as populações restantes.

fr.description: La colombine poignardée de Négros est une colombe terrestre nommée pour la vivante 'tache de sang' rouge sur sa poitrine blanche. Limitée aux îles de Negros et Panay aux Philippines, elle figure parmi les oiseaux les plus menacés d'Asie du Sud-Est.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation pour l'agriculture a éliminé plus de 95% de la forêt de basse altitude à Negros et Panay ; la forêt restante est intensément chassée, et l'espèce est également capturée pour le commerce d'oiseaux en cage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt primaire et secondaire mature intacte et non perturbée de basse altitude et de piémont à Negros et Panay, notamment des forêts avec un sous-bois dense, de la litière de feuilles pour la recherche de nourriture et une perturbation humaine minimale.
fr.actions: Soutenir la protection et la restauration forestière de la Fondation Haribon dans le parc naturel du nord de Negros, plaider pour des interdictions de chasse dans les fragments forestiers restants et financer des enquêtes pour localiser les populations restantes.

ja.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the 島s of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened 鳥 in 東南アジア.
ja.endangerment: De森林ation for 農業 has eliminated 以上 95% of lowland 森林 on Negros and Panay; remaining 森林 is heavily hunted, and the 種 is also captured for the cagebird 取引.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed 主要な and mature 二次的な lowland and foothill 森林 on Negros and Panay, particularly 森林 with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for 採取, and 最小限 human disturbance.
ja.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's 森林 保護ion and 修復 in the North Negros Natural Park, 提唱 for 狩猟 bans in remaining 森林 fragments, and fund surveys to 配置 any remaining 個体数s.

zh.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the 岛屿s of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened 鸟类 in 东南亚.
zh.endangerment: De森林ation for 农业 has eliminated 多于 95% of lowland 森林 on Negros and Panay; remaining 森林 is heavily hunted, and the 物种 is also captured for the cagebird 贸易.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill 森林 on Negros and Panay, particularly 森林 with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
zh.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's 森林 保护ion and 恢复 in the North Negros Natural Park, 倡导 for 狩猎 bans in remaining 森林 fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining 种群s.

ar.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the جزيرةs of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened طيور in جنوب شرق آسيا.
ar.endangerment: Deغابةation for الزراعة has eliminated أكثر من 95% of lowland غابة on Negros and Panay; remaining غابة is heavily hunted, and the الأنواع is also captured for the cagebird التجارة.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill غابة on Negros and Panay, particularly غابة with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
ar.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's غابة يحميion and إعادة in the North Negros Natural Park, يدافع for الصيد bans in remaining غابة fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining عدد السكانs.

hi.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the द्वीपs of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened पक्षी in दक्षिण-पूर्वी एशिया.
hi.endangerment: Deवनation for कृषि has eliminated अधिक से अधिक 95% of lowland वन on Negros and Panay; remaining वन is heavily hunted, and the प्रजातियाँ is also captured for the cagebird व्यापार.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill वन on Negros and Panay, particularly वन with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
hi.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's वन सुरक्षाion and पुनर्स्थापना in the North Negros Natural Park, समर्थन for शिकार bans in remaining वन fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining जनसंख्याs.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The Mississippi gopher frog is a stocky, warty frog that uses the burrows of gopher tortoises for shelter in longleaf pine forests of the US Gulf Coast. Fewer than 200 adults survive at a single natural breeding pond in Harrison County, Mississippi.
en.endangerment: The near-total loss of longleaf pine savanna and open-canopy forest — less than 3% of the original extent remains — combined with fire suppression that closes canopy over breeding ponds, has driven this species to the edge of extinction.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open-canopy longleaf pine savanna in the US Gulf Coastal Plain, with seasonal ephemeral ponds for breeding, sandy soils where gopher tortoises dig burrows for shelter, and regular fire maintaining open ground cover.
en.actions: Support the US Fish and Wildlife Service's Mississippi gopher frog recovery programme, fund longleaf pine restoration and prescribed burning at Harrison County, and back the head-starting programme releasing young frogs.

es.description: La rana topo de Mississippi es una rana rechoncha y verrugosa que usa las madrigueras de las tortugas topo para refugiarse en los bosques de pino de hoja larga de la costa del Golfo de EE.UU. Menos de 200 adultos sobreviven en un único estanque de cría natural en el Condado de Harrison, Mississippi.
es.endangerment: La pérdida casi total de la sabana de pino de hoja larga y el bosque de dosel abierto —menos del 3% de la extensión original queda— combinada con la supresión del fuego que cierra el dosel sobre los estanques de cría, ha llevado a esta especie al borde de la extinción.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Sabana de pino de hoja larga de dosel abierto en la Llanura Costera del Golfo de EE.UU., con estanques efímeros estacionales para la cría, suelos arenosos donde las tortugas topo excavan madrigueras para refugio y fuego regular que mantiene una cubierta vegetal abierta.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de recuperación de la rana topo de Mississippi del Servicio de Pesca y Vida Silvestre de EE.UU., financiar la restauración del pino de hoja larga y la quema prescrita en el Condado de Harrison y respaldar el programa de cría inicial que libera ranas jóvenes.

pt.description: A rã-toupeira-do-mississippi é uma rã rechonchuda e verrugosa que usa as tocas das tartarugas-toupeira para abrigo nas florestas de pinheiro-folha-longa da costa do Golfo dos EUA. Menos de 200 adultos sobrevivem em um único lago de reprodução natural no Condado de Harrison, Mississippi.
pt.endangerment: A perda quase total da savana de pinheiro-folha-longa e da floresta de dossel aberto — menos de 3% da extensão original permanece — combinada com a supressão do fogo que fecha o dossel sobre os lagos de reprodução, levou esta espécie à beira da extinção.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Savana de pinheiro-folha-longa de dossel aberto na Planície Costeira do Golfo dos EUA, com lagoas efêmeras sazonais para reprodução, solos arenosos onde as tartarugas-toupeira escavam tocas para abrigo e fogo regular mantendo uma cobertura vegetal aberta.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de recuperação da rã-toupeira-do-mississippi do Serviço de Pesca e Vida Silvestre dos EUA, financiar a restauração do pinheiro-folha-longa e a queima prescrita no Condado de Harrison e apoiar o programa de criação inicial que libera rãs jovens.

fr.description: La grenouille taupe du Mississippi est une grenouille trapue et verruqueuse qui utilise les terriers des tortues-taupes pour s'abriter dans les forêts de pin des marais de la côte du Golfe des États-Unis. Moins de 200 adultes survivent dans un seul étang de reproduction naturel dans le comté de Harrison, Mississippi.
fr.endangerment: La perte presque totale de la savane à pin des marais et de la forêt à canopée ouverte — moins de 3% de l'étendue originelle subsiste — combinée à la suppression des incendies qui ferment la canopée au-dessus des étangs de reproduction, a conduit cette espèce au bord de l'extinction.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Savane à canopée ouverte de pin des marais dans la plaine côtière du Golfe des États-Unis, avec des mares éphémères saisonnières pour la reproduction, des sols sableux où les tortues-taupes creusent des terriers pour l'abri et des incendies réguliers maintenant un couvert végétal ouvert.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de rétablissement de la grenouille taupe du Mississippi du Service des poissons et de la faune sauvage des États-Unis, financer la restauration du pin des marais et le brûlage dirigé dans le comté de Harrison et soutenir le programme d'élevage précoce libérant de jeunes grenouilles.

ja.ecosystemNeeds: Open-canopy longleaf pine savanna in the US Gulf 沿岸 Plain, with 季節 短命 ponds for breeding, sandy soils where gopher tortoises dig burrows for shelter, and regular fire maintaining open ground cover.
ja.actions: Support the US Fish and Wildlife Service's Mississippi gopher frog recovery プログラムme, fund longleaf pine 修復 and prescribed burning at Harrison County, and back the head-starting プログラムme releasing young frogs.

hi.endangerment: The near-total loss of longleaf pine savanna and open-canopy वन — less than 3% of the original extent remains — combined with fire suppression that closes canopy over breeding ponds, has driven this प्रजातियाँ to the edge of extinction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The purple frog is a blobby, dark-purple amphibian with a tiny pointed head, endemic to the Western Ghats of India. A relic lineage that separated from its closest relatives over 100 million years ago, it lives almost entirely underground, emerging only briefly to breed.
en.endangerment: Conversion of the Western Ghats' evergreen forests to monoculture plantations — particularly cardamom and coffee — destroys the deep, moist soil habitat where the frog spends 11 months of the year; chytridiomycosis is an emerging threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The deep laterite soil and leaf litter of semi-evergreen and moist deciduous forest in the Western Ghats, particularly at elevations of 100–1,600 metres in Tamil Nadu and Kerala, emerging only during monsoon to breed in streams.
en.actions: Support field surveys to map purple frog distribution across the Western Ghats, advocate for wildlife-friendly cardamom and coffee farming standards, and fund chytridiomycosis monitoring and antibiotic probiotic trials.

es.description: La rana púrpura es un anfibio de color púrpura oscuro y aspecto bulboso con una pequeña cabeza puntiaguda, endémico de los Ghats Occidentales de India. Un linaje relicto que se separó de sus parientes más cercanos hace más de 100 millones de años, vive casi enteramente bajo tierra, emergiendo solo brevemente para reproducirse.
es.endangerment: La conversión de los bosques siempreverdes de los Ghats Occidentales en plantaciones de monocultivo —particularmente cardamomo y café— destruye el hábitat de suelo profundo y húmedo donde la rana pasa 11 meses del año; la quitridiomicosis es una amenaza emergente.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El suelo profundo de laterita y la hojarasca del bosque semisiempreverde y caducifolio húmedo en los Ghats Occidentales, particularmente a altitudes de 100-1.600 metros en Tamil Nadu y Kerala, emergiendo solo durante el monzón para reproducirse en arroyos.
es.actions: Apoyar encuestas de campo para mapear la distribución de la rana púrpura en los Ghats Occidentales, abogar por estándares de cultivo de cardamomo y café favorables a la vida silvestre y financiar el monitoreo de la quitridiomicosis y ensayos de probióticos antibióticos.

pt.description: A rã-roxa é um anfíbio de cor roxo-escuro e aparência bulbosa com uma pequena cabeça pontiaguda, endêmico dos Ghats Ocidentais da Índia. Uma linhagem relíqua que se separou de seus parentes mais próximos há mais de 100 milhões de anos, vive quase inteiramente underground, emergindo apenas brevemente para se reproduzir.
pt.endangerment: A conversão das florestas perenes dos Ghats Ocidentais em plantações de monocultura — especialmente cardamomo e café — destrói o habitat de solo profundo e úmido onde a rã passa 11 meses do ano; a quitridiomicose é uma ameaça emergente.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O solo profundo de laterita e a serapilheira da floresta semi-perene e caducifólia úmida nos Ghats Ocidentais, particularmente em altitudes de 100-1.600 metros em Tamil Nadu e Kerala, emergindo apenas durante a monção para se reproduzir em córregos.
pt.actions: Apoiar levantamentos de campo para mapear a distribuição da rã-roxa nos Ghats Ocidentais, defender padrões de cultivo de cardamomo e café favoráveis à vida silvestre e financiar o monitoramento da quitridiomicose e ensaios probióticos antibióticos.

fr.description: La grenouille violette est un amphibien de couleur violet foncé et d'apparence bulbeuse à la petite tête pointue, endémique des Ghats occidentaux de l'Inde. Une lignée relique qui s'est séparée de ses plus proches parents il y a plus de 100 millions d'années, elle vit presque entièrement sous terre, n'émergeant que brièvement pour se reproduire.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des forêts sempervirentes des Ghats occidentaux en plantations de monoculture — notamment de cardamome et de café — détruit l'habitat de sol profond et humide où la grenouille passe 11 mois de l'année ; la chytridiomycose est une menace émergente.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Le sol de latérite profond et la litière de feuilles de la forêt semi-sempervirente et décidue humide dans les Ghats occidentaux, notamment à des altitudes de 100-1 600 mètres au Tamil Nadu et au Kerala, n'émergeant que pendant la mousson pour se reproduire dans les ruisseaux.
fr.actions: Soutenir des enquêtes de terrain pour cartographier la distribution de la grenouille violette dans les Ghats occidentaux, plaider pour des normes de culture de cardamome et de café favorables à la faune et financer la surveillance de la chytridiomycose et les essais de probiotiques antibiotiques.

imageCredit: Photo by Carlos Vasquez Almazan / Synchronicity Earth

en.description: Jackson's climbing salamander is a brilliantly coloured yellow-and-black amphibian that was known only from museum specimens for 42 years until its rediscovery on cloud forest trees in the Cuchumatanes Mountains of Guatemala in 2017.
en.endangerment: The cloud forest of the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in Huehuetenango is under intense pressure from illegal logging and slash-and-burn agriculture; chytridiomycosis threatens remaining populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid cloud forest at 2,600–2,850 metres in the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in northwestern Guatemala, spending its time in the leaf litter and on mossy tree trunks in undisturbed mature forest.
en.actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud forest protection programme in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic research, and advocate for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud forest as a protected area.

es.description: La salamandra trepadora de Jackson es un anfibio de color brillante amarillo y negro que solo era conocido por especímenes de museo durante 42 años hasta su redescubrimiento en los árboles de los bosques nubosos de las Montañas Cuchumatanes de Guatemala en 2017.
es.endangerment: El bosque nuboso de la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes en Huehuetenango está bajo intensa presión de la tala ilegal y la agricultura de tala y quema; la quitridiomicosis amenaza a las poblaciones restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque nuboso húmedo a 2.600-2.850 metros en la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes en el noroeste de Guatemala, pasando su tiempo en la hojarasca y en los troncos musgosos de los árboles en bosques maduros sin perturbaciones.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de protección del bosque nuboso de los Cuchumatanes de FUNDAECO en Guatemala, financiar investigaciones de probióticos anti-quitridio y abogar por la designación legal del bosque nuboso de los Cuchumatanes como área protegida.

pt.description: A salamandra-trepadora-de-jackson é um anfíbio de cores brilhantes amarelo e preto que era conhecido apenas por espécimes de museu por 42 anos até sua redescoberta nas árvores das florestas nebulares das Montanhas Cuchumatanes da Guatemala em 2017.
pt.endangerment: A floresta nebular da Sierra de los Cuchumatanes em Huehuetenango está sob intensa pressão do desmatamento ilegal e da agricultura de corte e queima; a quitridiomicose ameaça as populações restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta nebular úmida a 2.600-2.850 metros na Sierra de los Cuchumatanes no noroeste da Guatemala, passando seu tempo na serapilheira e nos troncos musgosos das árvores em florestas maduras sem perturbações.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de proteção da floresta nebular dos Cuchumatanes da FUNDAECO na Guatemala, financiar pesquisas de probióticos anti-quitrídio e defender a designação legal da floresta nebular dos Cuchumatanes como área protegida.

fr.description: La salamandre grimpante de Jackson est un amphibien brillamment coloré en jaune et noir qui n'était connu que par des spécimens de musée pendant 42 ans jusqu'à sa redécouverte dans les arbres de la forêt nuageuse des montagnes Cuchumatanes du Guatemala en 2017.
fr.endangerment: La forêt nuageuse de la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes à Huehuetenango est sous une intense pression de l'exploitation forestière illégale et de l'agriculture sur brûlis ; la chytridiomycose menace les populations restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt nuageuse humide à 2 600-2 850 mètres dans la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes dans le nord-ouest du Guatemala, passant son temps dans la litière et sur les troncs d'arbres moussus dans des forêts matures non perturbées.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de protection des forêts nuageuses des Cuchumatanes de FUNDAECO au Guatemala, financer des recherches sur les probiotiques anti-chytride et plaider pour la désignation légale de la forêt nuageuse des Cuchumatanes comme zone protégée.

ja.description: Jackson's 登山 salamander is a brilliantly coloured yellow-and-black amphibian that was 知るn only from museum specimens for 42 年s until its re発見y on cloud 森林 trees in the Cuchumatanes Mountains of Guatemala in 2017.
ja.endangerment: The cloud 森林 of the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in HuehuetenaNGO is under intense pressure from illegal 伐採 and slash-and-burn 農業; chytridiomycosis threatens remaining 個体数s.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Humid cloud 森林 at 2,600–2,850 metres in the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in northwestern Guatemala, spending its time in the leaf litter and on mossy tree trunks in undisturbed mature 森林.
ja.actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud 森林 保護ion プログラムme in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic 研究, and 提唱 for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud 森林 as a 保護ed area.

zh.actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud 森林 保护ion 项目me in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic 研究, and 倡导 for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud 森林 as a 保护ed area.

hi.description: Jackson's चढ़ाई salamander is a brilliantly coloured yellow-and-black amphibian that was जानेंn only from museum specimens for 42 years until its reखोजेंy on cloud वन trees in the Cuchumatanes Mountains of Guatemala in 2017.
hi.endangerment: The cloud वन of the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in Huehuetenaएनजीओ is under intense pressure from illegal लकड़ी काटना and slash-and-burn कृषि; chytridiomycosis threatens remaining जनसंख्याs.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Humid cloud वन at 2,600–2,850 metres in the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in northwestern Guatemala, spending its time in the leaf litter and on mossy tree trunks in undisturbed mature वन.
hi.actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud वन सुरक्षाion कार्यक्रमme in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic शोध, and समर्थन for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud वन as a सुरक्षाed area.

en.description: The Yangtze giant softshell turtle is the largest freshwater turtle on Earth, with a shell over a metre long. With fewer than four confirmed living individuals — including one female artificially inseminated in 2019 — it is among the most critically endangered animals on the planet.
en.endangerment: Hunting for food and traditional medicine, sand dredging, pollution, and dam construction across the Yangtze and Red River systems destroyed this turtle's habitat and population over the 20th century; the last confirmed wild female died in 2019.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep rivers and lakes in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red River drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on fish and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and pollution.
en.actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency captive breeding programme in China and Vietnam, advocate for environmental DNA surveys of the Red River system in Vietnam for any surviving individuals.

es.description: La tortuga de caparazón blando gigante del Yangtze es la tortuga de agua dulce más grande de la Tierra, con un caparazón de más de un metro de largo. Con menos de cuatro individuos vivos confirmados —incluyendo una hembra inseminada artificialmente en 2019— está entre los animales más críticamente amenazados del planeta.
es.endangerment: La caza para alimento y medicina tradicional, el dragado de arena, la contaminación y la construcción de presas en los sistemas del Yangtze y el Río Rojo destruyeron el hábitat y la población de esta tortuga durante el siglo XX; la última hembra silvestre confirmada murió en 2019.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos profundos y lagos en el Valle del Yangtze en China y la cuenca del Río Rojo en Vietnam, con playas arenosas para anidar, pozas profundas para alimentarse de peces y moluscos y mínimo tráfico de barcos y contaminación.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría de emergencia en cautiverio de la Turtle Survival Alliance en China y Vietnam, abogar por encuestas de ADN ambiental del sistema del Río Rojo en Vietnam para cualquier individuo superviviente.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-casca-mole-gigante-do-yangtze é a maior tartaruga de água doce da Terra, com um casco de mais de um metro de comprimento. Com menos de quatro indivíduos vivos confirmados — incluindo uma fêmea inseminada artificialmente em 2019 — está entre os animais mais criticamente ameaçados do planeta.
pt.endangerment: A caça para alimento e medicina tradicional, a dragagem de areia, a poluição e a construção de barragens nos sistemas do Yangtze e do Rio Vermelho destruíram o habitat e a população desta tartaruga ao longo do século XX; a última fêmea selvagem confirmada morreu em 2019.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios profundos e lagos no Vale do Yangtze na China e na bacia do Rio Vermelho no Vietnã, com praias arenosas para nidificação, poços profundos para se alimentar de peixes e moluscos e tráfego mínimo de barcos e poluição.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro de emergência da Turtle Survival Alliance na China e no Vietnã, defender levantamentos de DNA ambiental do sistema do Rio Vermelho no Vietnã para qualquer indivíduo sobrevivente.

fr.description: La tortue géante à carapace molle du Yangtsé est la plus grande tortue d'eau douce de la Terre, avec une carapace de plus d'un mètre de long. Avec moins de quatre individus vivants confirmés — dont une femelle inséminée artificiellement en 2019 — elle figure parmi les animaux les plus gravement menacés de la planète.
fr.endangerment: La chasse pour la nourriture et la médecine traditionnelle, le dragage du sable, la pollution et la construction de barrages sur les systèmes du Yangtsé et du fleuve Rouge ont détruit l'habitat et la population de cette tortue au cours du XXe siècle ; la dernière femelle sauvage confirmée est morte en 2019.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rivières profondes et lacs dans la vallée du Yangtsé en Chine et le bassin du fleuve Rouge au Vietnam, avec des plages sablonneuses pour la nidification, des fosses profondes pour se nourrir de poissons et de mollusques et un trafic de bateaux et une pollution minimaux.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage d'urgence en captivité de la Turtle Survival Alliance en Chine et au Vietnam, plaider pour des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental du système du fleuve Rouge au Vietnam pour tout individu survivant.

ja.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep 川s and 湖s in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red 河川 drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on 魚 and molluscs, and 最小限 boat traffic and 汚染.
ja.actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency 人死亡 プログラムme in China and Vietnam, 提唱 for 環境 DNA surveys of the Red 河川 system in Vietnam for any surviving 個体.

zh.description: The Yangtze giant softshell turtle is the 最大 淡水 turtle on Earth, with a shell over a metre long. With 少于 four confirmed living 个体 — including one female artificially inseminated in 2019 — it is among the most 极度 濒危 动物 on the 计划et.

zh.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep 河流s and 湖泊s in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red 河流 drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on 鱼类 and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and 污染.

ar.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep نهرs and بحيرةs in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red نهر drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on أسماك and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and التلوث.
ar.actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency التكاثر في الأسر برنامجme in China and Vietnam, يدافع for environmental DNA surveys of the Red نهر system in Vietnam for any surviving أفراد.

hi.description: The Yangtze giant softshell turtle is the सबसे बड़ा मीठा पानी turtle on Earth, with a shell over a metre long. With कम से कम four confirmed living व्यक्ति — including one female artificially inseminated in 2019 — it is among the most गंभीर रूप से लुप्तप्राय जानवर on the योजनाet.
hi.endangerment: Hunting for food and traditional medicine, sand dredging, प्रदूषण, and dam construction across the Yangtze and Red नदी systems destroyed this turtle's habitat and जनसंख्या over the 20th century; the last confirmed वन्य female died in 2019.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep नदीs and झीलs in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red नदी drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on मछली and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and प्रदूषण.
hi.actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency कैद प्रजनन कार्यक्रमme in China and Vietnam, समर्थन for environmental DNA surveys of the Red नदी system in Vietnam for any surviving व्यक्ति.

en.description: The spider tortoise is a small, attractively patterned tortoise endemic to the spiny dry forests of southwestern Madagascar. It can retract into its hinged shell and aestivate for months during drought, but its numbers have collapsed in recent decades.
en.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal international pet trade combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn agriculture in Madagascar's spiny forest has caused severe population declines.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny dry deciduous forest and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, dry leaf litter for foraging, and succulent plants for water.
en.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar programme, advocate for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund community patrol networks in spiny forest.

es.description: La tortuga araña es una pequeña tortuga de patrones atractivos endémica de los espinosos bosques secos del suroeste de Madagascar. Puede retraerse en su caparazón articulado y estivarse durante meses durante la sequía, pero sus números han colapsado en las últimas décadas.
es.endangerment: La caza furtiva para el comercio internacional ilegal de mascotas combinada con la destrucción del hábitat por la producción de carbón vegetal y la agricultura de tala y quema en el bosque espinoso de Madagascar ha causado graves declives de población.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque caducifolio seco y espinoso en las regiones de Tulear y Toliara del suroeste de Madagascar, con sustrato arenoso para excavar durante la estivación, hojarasca seca para forrajear y plantas suculentas para el agua.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa del suroeste de Madagascar del Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, abogar por la aplicación de la CITES y el fortalecimiento de las penas por el tráfico de tortugas y financiar redes de patrullas comunitarias en el bosque espinoso.

pt.description: A tartaruga-aranha é uma pequena tartaruga de padrões atraentes endêmica das florestas espinhosas secas do sudoeste de Madagascar. Pode se recolher em seu casco articulado e estivar por meses durante a seca, mas seus números colapsaram nas últimas décadas.
pt.endangerment: A caça furtiva para o comércio internacional ilegal de animais de estimação combinada com a destruição do habitat pela produção de carvão vegetal e pela agricultura de corte e queima na floresta espinhosa de Madagascar causou graves declínios populacionais.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta caducifólia seca e espinhosa nas regiões de Tulear e Toliara do sudoeste de Madagascar, com substrato arenoso para escavar durante a estivação, serapilheira seca para forragear e plantas suculentas para água.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa do sudoeste de Madagascar do Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, defender a aplicação da CITES e o fortalecimento das penalidades para o tráfico de tartarugas e financiar redes de patrulha comunitária na floresta espinhosa.

fr.description: La tortue araignée est une petite tortue aux motifs attrayants endémique des forêts sèches épineuses du sud-ouest de Madagascar. Elle peut se rétracter dans sa carapace articulée et s'estiver pendant des mois en période de sécheresse, mais ses effectifs se sont effondrés au cours des dernières décennies.
fr.endangerment: Le braconnage pour le commerce international illégal d'animaux de compagnie, combiné à la destruction de l'habitat par la production de charbon de bois et l'agriculture sur brûlis dans la forêt épineuse de Madagascar, a provoqué de graves déclins de population.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt sèche épineuse caduque dans les régions de Tulear et Toliara dans le sud-ouest de Madagascar, avec un substrat sableux pour s'enfouir pendant l'estivation, de la litière sèche de feuilles pour se nourrir et des plantes succulentes pour l'eau.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme du sud-ouest de Madagascar du Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, plaider pour l'application de la CITES et le renforcement des sanctions contre le trafic de tortues et financer des réseaux de patrouilles communautaires dans la forêt épineuse.

ja.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal 国際 pet 取引 combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn 農業 in Madagascar's spiny 森林 has caused severe 個体数 declines.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny 乾燥 deciduous 森林 and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for 穴掘り during aestivation, 乾燥 leaf litter for 採取, and succulent 植物 for water.
ja.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar プログラムme, 提唱 for CITES enforcement and 強化 penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund コミュニティ patrol networks in spiny 森林.

zh.description: The spider tortoise is a 小型, attractively patterned tortoise 特有 to the spiny 干燥 森林s of southwestern Madagascar. It can retract into its hinged shell and aestivate for months during drought, but its numbers have collapsed in recent decades.
zh.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal 国际 pet 贸易 combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn 农业 in Madagascar's spiny 森林 has caused severe 种群 declines.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny 干燥 deciduous 森林 and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, 干燥 leaf litter for foraging, and succulent 植物 for water.
zh.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar 项目me, 倡导 for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund 社区 patrol networks in spiny 森林.

ar.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal دولي pet التجارة combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn الزراعة in Madagascar's spiny غابة has caused severe عدد السكان declines.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny جاف deciduous غابة and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, جاف leaf litter for foraging, and succulent نباتات for water.
ar.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar برنامجme, يدافع for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund مجتمع patrol networks in spiny غابة.

hi.description: The spider tortoise is a छोटा, attractively patterned tortoise स्थानिक to the spiny सूखा वनs of southwestern Madagascar. It can retract into its hinged shell and aestivate for months during drought, but its numbers have collapsed in recent decades.
hi.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal अंतर्राष्ट्रीय pet व्यापार combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn कृषि in Madagascar's spiny वन has caused severe जनसंख्या declines.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny सूखा deciduous वन and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, सूखा leaf litter for foraging, and succulent पौधे for water.
hi.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar कार्यक्रमme, समर्थन for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund समुदाय patrol networks in spiny वन.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.

en.description: The Jamaican boa is Jamaica's largest terrestrial predator, a yellow-and-black constrictor reaching two metres. It controls rodent populations and pollinates flowers by serving as a nocturnal pollinator for certain plants. Persecution, road kills, and habitat loss have made it rare.
en.endangerment: Deliberate killing by humans who fear or dislike snakes, combined with predation of eggs and young by introduced mongooses and rats, and forest clearing for agriculture have severely reduced populations across Jamaica.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Native dry and wet limestone forest across Jamaica, particularly forest associated with limestone caves where it hunts birds and bats, large trees for roosting, and rocky areas for thermoregulation.
en.actions: Support Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust's boa education programme, advocate for legal protection against deliberate killing, and fund mongoose control in key boa habitat areas.

es.description: La boa jamaicana es el mayor depredador terrestre de Jamaica, un constrictor amarillo y negro que alcanza dos metros. Controla las poblaciones de roedores y poliniza flores sirviendo como polinizador nocturno de ciertas plantas. La persecución, las muertes por carretera y la pérdida de hábitat la han hecho escasa.
es.endangerment: La matanza deliberada por humanos que temen o no les gustan las serpientes, combinada con la depredación de huevos y crías por mangostas y ratas introducidas, y la tala forestal para la agricultura han reducido severamente las poblaciones en toda Jamaica.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosques nativos de piedra caliza seca y húmeda en toda Jamaica, particularmente bosque asociado con cuevas de piedra caliza donde caza aves y murciélagos, grandes árboles para descansar y áreas rocosas para la termorregulación.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa educativo sobre boas del Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, abogar por la protección legal contra la matanza deliberada y financiar el control de mangostas en las áreas clave de hábitat de boas.

pt.description: A jiboia-jamaicana é o maior predador terrestre da Jamaica, um construtor amarelo e preto que atinge dois metros. Controla as populações de roedores e poliniza flores servindo como polinizador noturno de certas plantas. A perseguição, as mortes na estrada e a perda de habitat a tornaram rara.
pt.endangerment: A matança deliberada por humanos que temem ou não gostam de cobras, combinada com a predação de ovos e filhotes por mangusto e ratos introduzidos, e o desmatamento para agricultura reduziram severamente as populações em toda a Jamaica.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Florestas nativas de calcário seco e úmido em toda a Jamaica, particularmente floresta associada a cavernas de calcário onde caça pássaros e morcegos, grandes árvores para descanso e áreas rochosas para termorregulação.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa educacional sobre jiboias do Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, defender a proteção legal contra a matança deliberada e financiar o controle do mangusto nas principais áreas de habitat da jiboia.

fr.description: Le boa de Jamaïque est le plus grand prédateur terrestre de la Jamaïque, un constricteur jaune et noir atteignant deux mètres. Il contrôle les populations de rongeurs et pollinise les fleurs en servant de pollinisateur nocturne pour certaines plantes. La persécution, les accidents de la route et la perte d'habitat l'ont rendu rare.
fr.endangerment: Le meurtre délibéré par des humains qui craignent ou n'aiment pas les serpents, combiné à la prédation des œufs et des jeunes par des mangoustes et des rats introduits, et le défrichement des forêts pour l'agriculture ont sévèrement réduit les populations à travers la Jamaïque.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêts calcaires sèches et humides indigènes à travers la Jamaïque, notamment les forêts associées aux grottes calcaires où il chasse les oiseaux et les chauves-souris, les grands arbres pour se reposer et les zones rocheuses pour la thermorégulation.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme éducatif sur les boas du Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, plaider pour une protection juridique contre le meurtre délibéré et financer le contrôle des mangoustes dans les zones clés d'habitat des boas.

ja.description: The Jamaican boa is Jamaica's 最大 地上 predator, a yellow-and-black constrictor reaching two metres. It controls rodent 個体数s and pollinates flowers by serving as a nocturnal pollinator for 確実 植物. Persecution, road kills, and 生息地の丧失 have made it まれ.

en.description: The four-eyed turtle gets its name from the two large, eye-like ocelli on the back of its head that may confuse predators. Native to mountainous streams of southern China and northern Vietnam, it is among the region's most traded turtles.
en.endangerment: Intensive collection for Chinese food and traditional medicine markets has devastated populations; habitat degradation from dam construction, sand mining, and stream channelisation has further reduced available habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Clear, fast-flowing, rocky mountain streams in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan, and Fujian in China, and northern Vietnam, with bankside vegetation and pools deep enough for foraging on aquatic invertebrates and small fish.
en.actions: Advocate for China's wildlife protection law enforcement against turtle markets, support WCS's turtle protection programme in southern China, and fund captive breeding populations as insurance against wild extinction.

es.description: La tortuga de cuatro ojos obtiene su nombre de los dos grandes ocelos en forma de ojo en la parte posterior de su cabeza que pueden confundir a los depredadores. Nativa de los arroyos de montaña del sur de China y el norte de Vietnam, está entre las tortugas más comercializadas de la región.
es.endangerment: La recolección intensiva para los mercados chinos de alimentos y medicina tradicional ha devastado las poblaciones; la degradación del hábitat por la construcción de presas, la minería de arena y la canalización de arroyos ha reducido aún más el hábitat disponible.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos de montaña rocosos, claros y de rápido flujo en Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan y Fujian en China, y el norte de Vietnam, con vegetación en las orillas y pozas lo suficientemente profundas para forrajear invertebrados acuáticos y peces pequeños.
es.actions: Abogar por la aplicación de la ley de protección de vida silvestre de China contra los mercados de tortugas, apoyar el programa de protección de tortugas de WCS en el sur de China y financiar poblaciones de cría en cautiverio como seguro contra la extinción silvestre.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-quatro-olhos recebe seu nome dos dois grandes ocelos em forma de olho na parte posterior de sua cabeça que podem confundir os predadores. Nativa dos córregos montanhosos do sul da China e norte do Vietnã, está entre as tartarugas mais comercializadas da região.
pt.endangerment: A coleta intensiva para os mercados chineses de alimentos e medicina tradicional devastou as populações; a degradação do habitat pela construção de barragens, mineração de areia e canalização de córregos reduziu ainda mais o habitat disponível.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Córregos de montanha rochosos, claros e de fluxo rápido em Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan e Fujian na China, e norte do Vietnã, com vegetação nas margens e poças profundas o suficiente para forragear invertebrados aquáticos e peixes pequenos.
pt.actions: Defender a aplicação da lei de proteção à vida silvestre da China contra os mercados de tartarugas, apoiar o programa de proteção de tartarugas do WCS no sul da China e financiar populações de criação em cativeiro como seguro contra a extinção selvagem.

fr.description: La tortue à quatre yeux doit son nom aux deux grands ocelles en forme d'yeux à l'arrière de sa tête qui peuvent dérouter les prédateurs. Originaire des ruisseaux de montagne du sud de la Chine et du nord du Vietnam, elle figure parmi les tortues les plus commercialisées de la région.
fr.endangerment: La collecte intensive pour les marchés alimentaires chinois et la médecine traditionnelle a dévasté les populations ; la dégradation de l'habitat due à la construction de barrages, à l'extraction de sable et à la canalisation des ruisseaux a encore réduit l'habitat disponible.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux de montagne rocheux, clairs et à courant rapide dans le Guangdong, le Guangxi, le Hainan et le Fujian en Chine, et dans le nord du Vietnam, avec une végétation en bord de berge et des mares suffisamment profondes pour chercher des invertébrés aquatiques et de petits poissons.
fr.actions: Plaider pour l'application de la loi chinoise sur la protection de la faune contre les marchés aux tortues, soutenir le programme de protection des tortues du WCS dans le sud de la Chine et financer des populations d'élevage en captivité comme assurance contre l'extinction sauvage.

hi.description: The four-eyed turtle gets its name from the two large, eye-like ocelli on the back of its head that may confuse predators. Native to पहाड़ous streams of southern China and northern Vietnam, it is among the region's most व्यापारd turtles.

en.description: The spotted handfish is a remarkable fish that "walks" across the seafloor on modified pectoral fins rather than swimming. Endemic to the lower Derwent Estuary in Tasmania, Australia, it is one of the world's most endangered marine fish.
en.endangerment: Introduction of the Pacific seastar has devastated populations by consuming the stalked ascidians the handfish uses as nest supports; siltation, pollution, and habitat degradation in the Derwent Estuary compound the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Sandy and silty seafloor of the lower Derwent River Estuary near Hobart, Tasmania, at depths of 1–30 metres, with stalked ascidians used as nest supports and abundant invertebrate prey.
en.actions: Support CSIRO's spotted handfish captive breeding programme, fund removal of invasive Pacific seastars from handfish habitat in the Derwent, and advocate for strict pollution controls in the Hobart urban estuary.

es.description: El pez mano manchado es un pez notable que 'camina' por el fondo del mar sobre aletas pectorales modificadas en lugar de nadar. Endémico del estuario bajo del Río Derwent en Tasmania, Australia, es uno de los peces marinos más amenazados del mundo.
es.endangerment: La introducción de la estrella de mar del Pacífico ha devastado las poblaciones al consumir las ascidias pedunculadas que el pez mano usa como soportes de nido; la sedimentación, la contaminación y la degradación del hábitat en el estuario Derwent agravan la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Fondo marino arenoso y limoso del estuario bajo del Río Derwent cerca de Hobart, Tasmania, a profundidades de 1-30 metros, con ascidias pedunculadas usadas como soportes de nido y abundante presa de invertebrados.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio del pez mano manchado del CSIRO, financiar la eliminación de estrellas de mar del Pacífico invasoras del hábitat del pez mano en el Derwent y abogar por estrictos controles de contaminación en el estuario urbano de Hobart.

pt.description: O peixe-mão-manchado é um peixe notável que 'caminha' pelo fundo do mar sobre nadadeiras peitorais modificadas em vez de nadar. Endêmico do estuário inferior do Rio Derwent na Tasmânia, Austrália, é um dos peixes marinhos mais ameaçados do mundo.
pt.endangerment: A introdução da estrela-do-mar-do-pacífico devastou as populações ao consumir as ascídias pedunculadas que o peixe-mão usa como suportes de ninho; o assoreamento, a poluição e a degradação do habitat no estuário Derwent agravam a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Fundo marinho arenoso e lodoso do estuário inferior do Rio Derwent perto de Hobart, Tasmânia, a profundidades de 1-30 metros, com ascídias pedunculadas usadas como suportes de ninho e abundante presa de invertebrados.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do peixe-mão-manchado do CSIRO, financiar a remoção de estrelas-do-mar-do-pacífico invasoras do habitat do peixe-mão no Derwent e defender controles rigorosos de poluição no estuário urbano de Hobart.

fr.description: Le poisson-main tacheté est un poisson remarquable qui 'marche' sur le fond marin sur des nageoires pectorales modifiées plutôt que de nager. Endémique du bas estuaire de la rivière Derwent en Tasmanie, en Australie, c'est l'un des poissons marins les plus menacés au monde.
fr.endangerment: L'introduction de l'étoile de mer du Pacifique a dévasté les populations en consommant les ascidies pédonculées que le poisson-main utilise comme supports de nid ; l'envasement, la pollution et la dégradation de l'habitat dans l'estuaire Derwent aggravent la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Fond marin sableux et limoneux du bas estuaire de la rivière Derwent près de Hobart, Tasmanie, à des profondeurs de 1-30 mètres, avec des ascidies pédonculées utilisées comme supports de nid et des proies invertébrées abondantes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité du poisson-main tacheté du CSIRO, financer la suppression des étoiles de mer du Pacifique envahissantes de l'habitat du poisson-main dans le Derwent et plaider pour des contrôles stricts de la pollution dans l'estuaire urbain de Hobart.

hi.description: The spotted handमछली is a remarkable मछली that "walks" across the seafloor on modified pectoral fins rather than तैराकी. Endemic to the lower Derwent Estuary in Tasmania, Australia, it is one of the विश्व's most लुप्तप्राय समुद्री मछली.

hi.ecosystemNeeds: Sandy and silty seafloor of the lower Derwent नदी Estuary near Hobart, Tasmania, at depths of 1–30 metres, with stalked ascidians used as nest सहायताs and प्रचुर invertebrate prey.
hi.actions: Support CSIRO's spotted handमछली कैद प्रजनन कार्यक्रमme, fund removal of invasive Pacific seastars from handमछली habitat in the Derwent, and समर्थन for strict प्रदूषण controls in the Hobart urban estuary.

en.description: The Siamese tiger perch is a large, boldly striped freshwater fish from the Chao Phraya and Mekong river systems in Thailand. Prized by aquarium collectors and as a food fish, it has virtually vanished from its native rivers.
en.endangerment: Overfishing for food and the aquarium trade, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water pollution in Thai river systems have reduced populations to near extinction in the wild.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing freshwater rivers with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush predation.
en.actions: Support Thai fisheries department captive breeding reintroduction work, advocate for stricter aquarium trade regulations, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai rivers to detect any remaining wild populations.

es.description: La perca tigre siamesa es un gran pez de agua dulce con llamativas rayas de los sistemas fluviales Chao Phraya y Mekong en Tailandia. Apreciado por los coleccionistas de acuarios y como pez alimenticio, ha desaparecido prácticamente de sus ríos nativos.
es.endangerment: La pesca excesiva para alimento y el comercio de acuarios, la construcción de presas que bloquean las rutas de migración y la severa contaminación del agua en los sistemas fluviales tailandeses han reducido las poblaciones casi a la extinción en la naturaleza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos de agua dulce de movimiento lento y profundo con estructura compleja incluyendo escombros sumergidos y sustrato rocoso en las cuencas del Chao Phraya y Mekong de Tailandia y Camboya, a profundidad media del agua para la depredación de emboscada.
es.actions: Apoyar el trabajo de reintroducción de cría en cautiverio del departamento de pesca tailandés, abogar por regulaciones más estrictas del comercio de acuarios y financiar encuestas de ADN ambiental en los ríos tailandeses para detectar cualquier población silvestre restante.

pt.description: A perca-tigre-siamesa é um grande peixe de água doce com listras chamativas dos sistemas fluviais Chao Phraya e Mekong na Tailândia. Apreciado por colecionadores de aquário e como peixe alimentar, praticamente desapareceu de seus rios nativos.
pt.endangerment: A pesca excessiva para alimento e o comércio de aquário, a construção de barragens bloqueando rotas migratórias e a severa poluição da água nos sistemas fluviais tailandeses reduziram as populações quase à extinção na natureza.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios de água doce de movimento lento e profundos com estrutura complexa incluindo detritos submersos e substrato rochoso nas bacias do Chao Phraya e Mekong da Tailândia e Camboja, a profundidade média da água para predação de emboscada.
pt.actions: Apoiar o trabalho de reintrodução de criação em cativeiro do departamento de pesca tailandês, defender regulamentações mais rigorosas do comércio de aquário e financiar levantamentos de DNA ambiental nos rios tailandeses para detectar qualquer população selvagem restante.

fr.description: La perche tigre siamoise est un grand poisson d'eau douce aux rayures frappantes des systèmes fluviaux Chao Phraya et Mékong en Thaïlande. Prisée par les collectionneurs d'aquarium et comme poisson alimentaire, elle a pratiquement disparu de ses rivières natales.
fr.endangerment: La surpêche pour la nourriture et le commerce d'aquarium, la construction de barrages bloquant les voies migratoires et la grave pollution de l'eau dans les systèmes fluviaux thaïlandais ont réduit les populations à la quasi-extinction à l'état sauvage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rivières d'eau douce à courant lent et profondes avec une structure complexe comprenant des débris submergés et des substrats rocheux dans les bassins du Chao Phraya et du Mékong en Thaïlande et au Cambodge, à mi-profondeur pour la prédation en embuscade.
fr.actions: Soutenir les travaux de réintroduction par élevage en captivité du département des pêches thaïlandais, plaider pour des réglementations plus strictes du commerce d'aquarium et financer des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental dans les rivières thaïlandaises pour détecter les populations sauvages restantes.

ja.description: The Siamese tiger perch is a large, boldly striped 淡水 魚 from the Chao Phraya and Mekong 川 systems in Thailand. Prized by aquarium collectors and as a food 魚, it has virtually vanished from its native 川s.
ja.endangerment: Over魚ing for food and the aquarium 取引, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water 汚染 in Thai 川 systems have 削減d 個体数s to near 絶滅 in the 野生.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing 淡水 川s with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush 捕食.
ja.actions: Support Thai 魚eries department 人死亡 再導入 work, 提唱 for stricter aquarium 取引 規制s, and fund 環境 DNA surveys in Thai 川s to detect any remaining 野生 個体数s.

zh.description: The Siamese tiger perch is a large, boldly striped 淡水 鱼类 from the Chao Phraya and Mekong 河流 systems in Thailand. Prized by aquarium collectors and as a food 鱼类, it has virtually vanished from its native 河流s.
zh.endangerment: Over鱼类ing for food and the aquarium 贸易, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water 污染 in Thai 河流 systems have 减少d 种群s to near extinction in the 野生.
zh.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing 淡水 河流s with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush 捕食.
zh.actions: Support Thai 鱼类eries department 圈养繁殖 重新引入 work, 倡导 for stricter aquarium 贸易 法规s, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai 河流s to detect any remaining 野生 种群s.

ar.actions: Support Thai أسماكeries department التكاثر في الأسر إعادة الإدخال work, يدافع for stricter aquarium التجارة اللائحةs, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai نهرs to detect any remaining بري عدد السكانs.

hi.endangerment: Overमछलीing for food and the aquarium व्यापार, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water प्रदूषण in Thai नदी systems have कमd जनसंख्याs to near extinction in the वन्य.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing मीठा पानी नदीs with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush शिकार.
hi.actions: Support Thai मछलीeries department कैद प्रजनन पुनर्प्रवर्तन work, समर्थन for stricter aquarium व्यापार विनियमs, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai नदीs to detect any remaining वन्य जनसंख्याs.

en.description: The Brazilian guitarfish is a large elasmobranch with a body shaped between a shark and a ray, once abundant in the warm shallow coastal waters of southern Brazil and Uruguay. Industrial fishing has reduced it by more than 80% in recent decades.
en.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline fisheries, combined with targeted fishing for its large fins, has devastated populations; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by coastal development.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow coastal waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic coast of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and abundant benthic invertebrates and small fish for adults.
en.actions: Support WWF-Brazil's marine elasmobranch programme, advocate for guitarfish-specific bycatch reduction measures in Brazilian shrimp fisheries, and fund population surveys along the southern Brazilian coast.

es.description: La guitarra brasileña es un gran elasmobranquio con un cuerpo en forma de entre un tiburón y una raya, antes abundante en las aguas costeras cálidas y poco profundas del sur de Brasil y Uruguay. La pesca industrial lo ha reducido en más del 80% en las últimas décadas.
es.endangerment: La captura accidental de alto valor en arrastres de camarón y pesquerías de palangre, combinada con la pesca dirigida por sus grandes aletas, ha devastado las poblaciones; los estuarios poco profundos y las áreas de cría que necesita para las crías están muy impactados por el desarrollo costero.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras poco profundas, estuarios y bahías arenosas a lo largo de la costa atlántica del sur de Brasil y Uruguay, con praderas de posidonia como hábitat de cría para las crías y abundantes invertebrados bentónicos y peces pequeños para los adultos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de elasmobranquios marinos de WWF-Brasil, abogar por medidas específicas de reducción de captura accidental de guitarra en las pesquerías de camarón brasileñas y financiar encuestas de población a lo largo de la costa sur de Brasil.

pt.description: O violão-brasileiro é um grande elasmobrânquio com um corpo em forma de entre um tubarão e uma arraia, antes abundante nas águas costeiras quentes e rasas do sul do Brasil e Uruguai. A pesca industrial o reduziu em mais de 80% nas últimas décadas.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental de alto valor em redes de arrasto de camarão e pesqueiras de espinhel, combinada com a pesca direcionada por suas grandes barbatanas, devastou as populações; os estuários rasos e as áreas de criação que necessita para os filhotes são intensamente impactados pelo desenvolvimento costeiro.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras rasas, estuários e baías arenosas ao longo da costa atlântica do sul do Brasil e Uruguai, com pradarias de ervas marinhas como habitat de criação para filhotes e abundantes invertebrados bentônicos e peixes pequenos para adultos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de elasmobranquios marinhos do WWF-Brasil, defender medidas específicas de redução de captura acidental de violão nas pesqueiras de camarão brasileiras e financiar levantamentos populacionais ao longo da costa sul do Brasil.

fr.description: La raie-guitare brésilienne est un grand élasmobranches au corps en forme de hybride entre un requin et une raie, autrefois abondant dans les eaux côtières chaudes et peu profondes du sud du Brésil et de l'Uruguay. La pêche industrielle l'a réduit de plus de 80% au cours des dernières décennies.
fr.endangerment: Les captures accessoires de grande valeur dans les chaluts à crevettes et les palangres, combinées à la pêche ciblée pour ses grandes nageoires, ont dévasté les populations ; les estuaires peu profonds et les zones de nurserie dont il a besoin pour les jeunes sont fortement impactés par le développement côtier.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux côtières peu profondes, estuaires et baies sablonneuses le long de la côte atlantique du sud du Brésil et de l'Uruguay, avec des herbiers marins comme habitat de nurserie pour les jeunes et des invertébrés benthiques abondants et de petits poissons pour les adultes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élasmobranches marins du WWF-Brésil, plaider pour des mesures spécifiques de réduction des prises accessoires de raies-guitares dans les pêcheries brésiliennes de crevettes et financer des enquêtes sur les populations le long de la côte sud du Brésil.

ja.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline 魚eries, combined with targeted 魚ing for its large fins, has devastated 個体数s; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by 海岸al 開発.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow 海岸al waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic 海岸 of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and 豊富 benthic 無脊椎動物 and 小さい 魚 for adults.
ja.actions: Support WWF-Brazil's 海洋 elasmobranch プログラムme, 提唱 for guitar魚-specific bycatch reduction measures in Brazilian shrimp 魚eries, and fund 個体数 surveys along the southern Brazilian 海岸.

zh.description: The Brazilian guitar鱼类 is a large elasmobranch with a body shaped 之间 a shark and a ray, once 丰富 in the 温暖 shallow 海岸al waters of southern Brazil and Uruguay. Industrial 鱼类ing has 减少d it by 多于 80% in recent decades.
zh.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline 鱼类eries, combined with targeted 鱼类ing for its large fins, has devastated 种群s; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by 海岸al 开发.

ar.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline أسماكeries, combined with targeted أسماكing for its large fins, has devastated عدد السكانs; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by ساحلal التطوير.
ar.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow ساحلal waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic ساحل of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and وفير benthic لافقاريات and صغير أسماك for adults.
ar.actions: Support WWF-Brazil's بحري elasmobranch برنامجme, يدافع for guitarأسماك-specific bycatch reduction measures in Brazilian shrimp أسماكeries, and fund عدد السكان surveys along the southern Brazilian ساحل.

hi.description: The Brazilian guitarमछली is a large elasmobranch with a body shaped के बीच a shark and a ray, once प्रचुर in the गर्म shallow तटal waters of southern Brazil and Uruguay. Industrial मछलीing has कमd it by अधिक से अधिक 80% in recent decades.
hi.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline मछलीeries, combined with targeted मछलीing for its large fins, has devastated जनसंख्याs; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by तटal विकास.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow तटal waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic तट of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and प्रचुर benthic अकशेरुकी and छोटा मछली for adults.

en.description: The monkey puzzle tree is a living fossil conifer from the Andes and coastal mountains of Chile and Argentina, with interlocking triangular leaves that persist for up to 24 years. Sacred to the Pehuenche people, its large seeds — piñones — are a critical food source.
en.endangerment: Past commercial logging devastated old-growth stands; now the Patagonian fire cycle, amplified by climate change, and invasive exotic species threaten remaining populations; cattle grazing prevents regeneration in many areas.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and Coastal Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a cold, snowy climate with high annual rainfall.
en.actions: Support CONAF's monkey puzzle protected area network in the Araucanía region, advocate for a complete ban on monkey puzzle felling, and fund reforestation with locally sourced seeds in degraded Andean stands.

es.description: El árbol rompecabezas de mono es un conífera fósil viviente de los Andes y las montañas costeras de Chile y Argentina, con hojas triangulares entrelazadas que persisten hasta 24 años. Sagrado para el pueblo Pehuenche, sus grandes semillas —piñones— son una fuente crítica de alimento.
es.endangerment: La tala comercial pasada devastó los rodales de bosque antiguo; ahora el ciclo de incendios patagónico, amplificado por el cambio climático, y las especies exóticas invasoras amenazan las poblaciones restantes; el pastoreo de ganado impide la regeneración en muchas áreas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Suelos volcánicos y laderas rocosas de los Andes y la Cordillera Costera a 900-1.800 metros en las regiones de Araucanía y Bío Bío de Chile y la provincia de Neuquén de Argentina, en un clima frío y nevado con alta precipitación anual.
es.actions: Apoyar la red de áreas protegidas del árbol rompecabezas de mono de CONAF en la región de Araucanía, abogar por una prohibición total de la tala del árbol rompecabezas de mono y financiar la reforestación con semillas de origen local en los rodales andinos degradados.

pt.description: A araucária é uma conífera fóssil viva dos Andes e das montanhas costeiras do Chile e Argentina, com folhas triangulares entrelaçadas que persistem por até 24 anos. Sagrada para o povo Pehuenche, suas grandes sementes — piñones — são uma fonte crítica de alimento.
pt.endangerment: A exploração comercial passada devastou os povoamentos de floresta antiga; agora o ciclo de incêndios patagônico, amplificado pelas mudanças climáticas, e as espécies exóticas invasoras ameaçam as populações restantes; o pastejo de gado impede a regeneração em muitas áreas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Solos vulcânicos e encostas rochosas dos Andes e da Cordilheira Costeira a 900-1.800 metros nas regiões de Araucanía e Bío Bío do Chile e na província de Neuquén da Argentina, em um clima frio e nevado com alta precipitação anual.
pt.actions: Apoiar a rede de áreas protegidas da araucária da CONAF na região de Araucanía, defender uma proibição total do corte da araucária e financiar o reflorestamento com sementes de origem local nos povoamentos andinos degradados.

fr.description: L'araucaria du Chili est un conifère fossile vivant des Andes et des montagnes côtières du Chili et de l'Argentine, avec des feuilles triangulaires imbriquées qui persistent jusqu'à 24 ans. Sacré pour le peuple Pehuenche, ses grandes graines — piñones — constituent une source d'alimentation critique.
fr.endangerment: L'exploitation commerciale passée a dévasté les peuplements de vieille forêt ; le cycle d'incendies patagonien, amplifié par le changement climatique, et les espèces exotiques envahissantes menacent maintenant les populations restantes ; le pâturage du bétail empêche la régénération dans de nombreuses zones.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Sols volcaniques et pentes rocheuses des Andes et de la Cordillère côtière à 900-1 800 mètres dans les régions d'Araucanía et de Bío Bío au Chili et dans la province de Neuquén en Argentine, dans un climat froid et enneigé avec de fortes précipitations annuelles.
fr.actions: Soutenir le réseau de zones protégées de l'araucaria de la CONAF dans la région d'Araucanía, plaider pour une interdiction totale de l'abattage de l'araucaria et financer le reboisement avec des graines d'origine locale dans les peuplements andins dégradés.

ja.endangerment: Past commercial 伐採 devastated old-growth stands; now the Patagonian fire cycle, amplified by 気候変動, and invasive exotic 種 threaten remaining 個体数s; cattle 採草 prevents regeneration in many areas.
ja.ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and 沿岸 Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a 寒い, snowy climate with high 一年生 rainfall.
ja.actions: Support CONAF's monkey puzzle 保護ed area network in the Araucanía region, 提唱 for a complete ban on monkey puzzle felling, and fund re森林ation with 局的ly sourced seeds in degraded Andean stands.

ar.ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and ساحلي Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a بارد, snowy climate with high annual rainfall.

hi.endangerment: Past commercial लकड़ी काटना devastated old-growth stands; now the Patagonian fire cycle, amplified by जलवायु परिवर्तन, and invasive exotic प्रजातियाँ threaten remaining जनसंख्याs; cattle grazing prevents regeneration in many areas.
hi.ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and तटीय Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a ठंडा, snowy climate with high annual rainfall.
